questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates the beginning of a zone with a maximum speed of 30 km/h. It is often set up in residential areas and only once at the beginning. You are not allowed to drive faster than 30 km / h in the entire area. This speed limit is canceled with the "End of the 30 km/h zone" sign.
answerASource: - a permitted maximum speed of 30 km/h
answerBSource: - an obligatory minimum speed of 30 km/h
answerCSource: - a recommended speed of 30 km/h

questionTextSource: A traffic calmed commercial zone (verkehrsberuhigter Bereich) starts here. What must you do?
explanationSource: In a traffic calming zone with shops, road users are often distracted by the displays in the shop windows. You must therefore pay attention to rain and inattentive foot traffic. Your maximum speed is 20 km/h. This is a no parking, but waiting allowed zone and you're obliged to use your parking disc. To offer parking to many customers, the maximum parking time is two hours.
answerASource: You must specifically watch out for busy pedestrian traffic
answerBSource: You must pay attention to the allowed maximum parking time
answerCSource: You must observe a minimum speed of 20 km/h

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates the end of an area in which the permitted speed was set to 30 km/h and thus cancels the maximum speed of 30 km/h.
answerASource: - a permitted maximum speed of 30 km/h
answerBSource: - an obligatory minimum speed of 30 km/h
answerCSource: - a recommended speed of 30 km/h

questionTextSource: Are you allowed to drive at a speed faster than 60 km/h here?
explanationSource: Due to the additional sign, the maximum permissible speed of 60 km/h applies only in wet conditions. Decrease the speed of your vehicle accordingly, if the entire roadway is covered with a thin film of water and therefore counts as wet. If the road is dry or only damp, you are also allowed to drive faster than 60 km/h as a car driver.
answerASource: Yes, if the roadway is dry
answerBSource: Yes, if the roadway is wet

questionTextSource: What do you do when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign dictates that you have to drive here at least at a speed of 60 km/h. Driving slower is only allowed if traffic safety requires it. A car driver may not use this road if he/she cannot or is not allowed to drive that fast due to the vehicle or its load or due to lack of personal abilities.
answerASource: You must drive at least at a speed of 60 km/h, weather and traffic conditions permitting
answerBSource: You may not use this road if you cannot or are not allowed to drive at least 60 km/h
answerCSource: You are not allowed to drive faster than 60 km/h

questionTextSource: Which vehicles are you allowed to overtake where you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The restriction on overtaking prohibits a car driver from overtaking multi-lane vehicles such as cars, trucks or tractors and motorcycles with side cars. Motorcycles without a side car and bicycles are allowed to be overtaken, as these are single-lane vehicles.
answerASource: Motorcycle without sidecar
answerBSource: Motorcycle with sidecar
answerCSource: Car

questionTextSource: What do these traffic signs indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign signals a restriction on overtaking on multi-lane motor vehicles or motorcycles with side car. The black arrows with the km indication indicate that the restriction on overtaking applies for 3 km. The restriction on overtaking comes into force starting from the traffic sign.
answerASource: No overtaking for 3 km
answerBSource: Start of a no-overtaking area 3 km ahead
answerCSource: Roadway with two lanes in one direction for 3 km

questionTextSource: What do these traffic signs indicate?
explanationSource: With this traffic sign the car driver is prohibited to overtake multi-lane vehicles and motorcycles with side car. The additional sign prohibits overtaking in 200 meters. Since it contains no black arrows, it does not mean that you are not allowed to overtake in the next 200 meters.
answerASource: Start of a no-overtaking area 200 m ahead
answerBSource: No overtaking for 200 m
answerCSource: End of a no-overtaking area 200 m ahead

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates the end of the speed limit of 60 km/h. The car driver may drive at the usual speed again.
answerASource: End of speed restriction
answerBSource: A prohibition to drive faster than 60 km/h
answerCSource: Start of speed restriction

questionTextSource: Which prohibitions cease to apply when you see this sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign cancels all road restrictions. For example, road restrictions include restrictions on overtaking and speed limits. Stopping restrictions such as a parking ban are not covered by road restrictions.
answerASource: No overtaking
answerBSource: Speed restrictions
answerCSource: No parking

questionTextSource: What do these traffic signs indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign signals the car driver that it is forbidden to come to a stop on the road. The additional sign extends the stopping restriction and also prohibits stopping on the side strip for all vehicles, not just for trucks.
answerASource: - on the roadway and the hard shoulder
answerBSource: - on the hard shoulder only
answerCSource: - for trucks only

questionTextSource: What is allowed in the zone which starts here?
explanationSource: With the traffic sign "No parking. Waiting is allowed.", there is a restricted stopping ban in an entire area without stopping signs being placed here in certain streets. As a car driver, you are allowed to wait up to three minutes throughout the entire zone. Situations of loading and unloading or getting in and out of the car may also take longer.
answerASource: Stopping for up to 3 minutes
answerBSource: Stopping for loading or unloading as well as boarding or alighting
answerCSource: Parking, provided a parking disc is used

questionTextSource: What ends here?
explanationSource: Here the no parking zone ends. Note that this traffic sign is often only on placed on the left side of the road and does not suspend the stopping restriction.
answerASource: A no-waiting area
answerBSource: A no-stopping area
answerCSource: All previous zonal prohibitions

questionTextSource: When must you indicate?
explanationSource: If you enter the roundabout, you do not need to indicate this because the entrance to the circle is always to the right. On the other hand, if you leave the roundabout, you will need to indicate this to make it easier for other drivers to enter the circle.
answerASource: When you leave the roundabout
answerBSource: When you enter the roundabout

questionTextSource: You are approaching this combination of traffic signs. What must you remember?
explanationSource: Vehicles driving in the roundabout have right of way. Indicating when entering the roundabout is not necessary, as a roundabout is always entered from the right. Therefore, the answer "Entering the roundabout must be indicated" is wrong. You only indicate when you leave a roundabout. This makes sense because this makes it easier for vehicles to enter the roundabout.
answerASource: Vehicles in the roundabout have priority
answerBSource: You must indicate to leave the roundabout
answerCSource: You must indicate when entering the roundabout

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign mean?
explanationSource: You have to give priority to the vehicles driving in the roundabout. In the roundabout there is always an absolute stopping restriction. You only indicate when you leave a roundabout. This helps other cars to enter the roundabout. A general driving with walking speed in the roundabout is not required.
answerASource: Stopping on the roundabout is forbidden
answerBSource: You must indicate when entering the roundabout
answerCSource: You are only allowed to drive at walking speed

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign mean?
explanationSource: The traffic signs indicate that the drivers in the roundabout have priority. They also show that you do not need to indicate when entering the roundabout, as motorists can only enter the circle to the right. As soon as you leave the roundabout, you will need to indicate that to make it easier for other road users to enter the roundabout.
answerASource: You must give way to vehicles in the roundabout
answerBSource: You must indicate when leaving the roundabout
answerCSource: You must indicate when entering the roundabout

questionTextSource: What must you do when you see this combination of traffic signs?
explanationSource: When driving in, you must give priority to the vehicles in that are already in the roundabout. The circle may only be entered through the right. You only indicate when leaving the roundabout. This regulation makes it easier for drivers to enter the roundabout. When recognizing the turn signal, they can decide whether they can already enter the circle with due regard for due diligence.
answerASource: You must indicate right to leave the roundabout
answerBSource: When entering the roundabout, you must give way
answerCSource: You must enter to the left

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign identifies a cycle path that cyclists must use but is prohibited for other road users. Cyclists crossing the road have their own warning sign.
answerASource: A mandatory cycle path
answerBSource: A "cyclists prohibited" zone
answerCSource: Cyclists crossing the carriageway

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign combination indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign announces that after a hundred meters you will find a priority road where you will find the traffic sign "Give way". In any case, you must stop there at the stop line or line of sight.
answerASource: - "Stop. Give way."
answerBSource: - "Give way."
answerCSource: - "Give way to oncoming traffic"

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign "Footway" marks paths which may be used exclusively by pedestrians and cyclists up to 10 years. Car drivers, cyclists and other road users may not be traveling on these routes. However, exceptions may be regulated by additional signs.
answerASource: A footpath
answerBSource: A prohibition on cycles and mopeds
answerCSource: The start of a traffic-calming zone

questionTextSource: What vehicles are permitted to use this road?
explanationSource: The traffic sign "No vehicles of any kind permitted" includes all vehicles. Only motor bicycles such as motorcycles and scooters and bicycles may be pushed here.
answerASource: Cycles and motorcycles that are pushed
answerBSource: Motor vehicles
answerCSource: Electric-powered vehicles

questionTextSource: How must you act when driving a vehicle during permitted delivery times?
explanationSource: Since the traffic sign "Footway" is combined here with the additional sign "Delivery vehicles only", a distributor may drive on a road that is marked this way. You have to drive at walking speed, regardless of whether pedestrians are visible or not. Pedestrians have priority, you should be especially considerate of them.
answerASource: I may only drive at walking speed
answerBSource: I must keep a close look out for pedestrians
answerCSource: If there are no pedestrians in my vicinity, I may drive faster than walking speed

questionTextSource: What must you do upon seeing this traffic sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates the prohibition to turn in order to drive in the opposite direction. Turning left, however, is allowed. This traffic sign does not only apply to dead ends.
answerASource: I may not perform a U-turn here
answerBSource: I am in a cul-de-sac and have to turn around
answerCSource: I may not turn left here

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: Starting from these traffic signs a maximum of 60 km/h may be driven, regardless of the weather conditions. In bad weather conditions, the speed must be reduced further. However, the traffic sign does not say that 60 km/h must always be driven in order to stay safe. There is no traffic sign for a recommended minimum speed.
answerASource: I may not drive faster than 60 km/h
answerBSource: There is absolutely no problem in driving at 60 km/h
answerCSource: The recommended minimum speed is 60 km/h

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign means "You must go straight ahead". You can only drive straight ahead and are not allowed to turn. The one-way street sign has the same colors but is rectangular.
answerASource: I may only drive straight ahead
answerBSource: I have priority over oncoming traffic
answerCSource: I am on a one-way road

questionTextSource: What do you do upon seeing this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The traffic signal "Give way" signals that you are driving on a subordinate road and the cross traffic has priority. Drive slowly and stay ready to brake so that you do not miss a vehicle that is entitled to drive.
answerASource: I approach at a moderate speed ready to brake
answerBSource: I allow the cross-wise moving traffic the right of way
answerCSource: If I am turning right, I am not obliged to wait

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign shows you a riding path. Here riders move with their horses. Other road users are not allowed to use this path.
answerASource: A bridle-path, which riders are required to use
answerBSource: A bridle-path, which may not be used by other road users
answerCSource: A path, which may not be used by riders

questionTextSource: What must you do upon seeing this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The sign "Stop! Yield to cross traffic" requires you to stop and thereby slow down the vehicle to a complete stop. It doesn't matter if there is a stop line or if cross traffic is coming.
answerASource: - stop and give way
answerBSource: - stop only if there is a stop line
answerCSource: - stop only if there is cross-wise moving traffic

questionTextSource: What do you have to do in this traffic situation?
explanationSource: At the traffic sign "Stop! Yield to cross traffic" you have to stop and give way. You always have to stop, even if there is no cross traffic. Since there is no stop line, stop at the line of sight. From the line of sight, you can see the street you want to enter.
answerASource: - stop at the line of sight and give way
answerBSource: - not stop, because there is no stop line
answerCSource: - stop only if there is cross-wise moving traffic

questionTextSource: What do you have to do in this traffic situation?
explanationSource: The traffic sign "Stop! Yield to cross traffic!" requires that you stop and give way. Since there is a stop line, you must stop at the stop line. If there is no stop line, you must stop at the line of sight and your wheels have to stop completely. You always have to stop, even if there is no cross traffic.
answerASource: - stop at the stop line and give way
answerBSource: - stop only at the stop sign
answerCSource: - stop only if there is cross-wise moving traffic

questionTextSource: You are driving along a priority road. Which traffic sign ends your right of way?
explanationSource: Example 1 means "Yield", you must give priority to other road users. Sign 2 grants you right of way at the next intersection. Sign 3 only warns of a danger zone.

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of with this traffic sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign regulates the direction of travel. You may only turn left here. The change of the direction of travel must be indicated.
answerASource: I have to signal a left-turn
answerBSource: I may only continue driving by taking a left turn
answerCSource: I may not signal

questionTextSource: What applies with this traffic sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign prescribes from which side you have to circuit the obstacle. You can find the regulation sign at central, stop islands or construction sites. If you meet the sign at an intersection or T-junction, you may turn in the appropriate direction.
answerASource: I have to drive past obstructions on the left
answerBSource: I am obliged to turn left
answerCSource: I may not turn right before the sign

questionTextSource: What applies in the area 15 m in front of and behind this traffic sign?
explanationSource: At the traffic sign you can see that there is a tram or bus stop here. 15 meters before and after the traffic sign, drivers are not allowed to park their vehicles. Unless public transport is impeded or passengers are endangered, waiting is allowed for up to three minutes.
answerASource: - stop
answerBSource: - not obstruct passengers boarding or alighting
answerCSource: - park

questionTextSource: What applies with this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The given direction of travel is binding. The car driver must turn right before the traffic sign and indicate this process to other road users.
answerASource: I have to signal a right-turn
answerBSource: I may only continue driving by taking a right turn
answerCSource: I may also continue driving by taking a left turn

questionTextSource: What applies with this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates a taxi stand. There is basically a stopping restriction for all other vehicles. The car driver must always expect departing and arriving taxis and passengers in this area.
answerASource: I have to anticipate taxi moving off and coming to a stop
answerBSource: I may stop
answerCSource: I may park

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign combination indicate?
explanationSource: This sign means "Danger zone" and the additional sign stands for "Winter sports allowed". The combination indicates that winter sportspersons may use the road and road users must pay particular attention to it.
answerASource: - may be used by winter sports enthusiasts
answerBSource: - may not be used by traffic in winter
answerCSource: - may only be used with snow chains

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of in this traffic situation?
explanationSource: You must not drive over the lane boundary (solid line) to the left, which is located at the middle of the lane. Driving on it is also prohibited.
answerASource: - not cross the lane marking
answerBSource: - cross the lane marking in order to overtake
answerCSource: - cross the lane marking if oncoming traffic permits this

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of on this priority road?
explanationSource: The solid line in the middle between the two lanes prohibits crossing or crossing this line. It also prohibits parking on priority roads outside of built-up areas.
answerASource: Parking is prohibited
answerBSource: You may not cross the lane marking
answerCSource: Parking is permitted if there is a clear space of at least 3 m between a parked car and the lane marking

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What is the right thing to do?
explanationSource: You may overtake if other road users are not endangered. The one-sided lane boundary allows you to cross the two lines from the side where the broken line is located.
answerASource: - overtake
answerBSource: - not overtake
answerCSource: - only overtake, if I remain in the right-hand lane while doing so

questionTextSource: As a resident, you want to drive into a cycle highway that has this sign. What must you be aware of when doing so?
explanationSource: A maximum speed of 30 km/h applies to traffic. Cycling traffic must not be endangered or obstructed. If necessary, motor vehicle traffic must reduce speed further. Side-by-side cycling is permitted.
answerASource: Vehicles may not be driven faster than 30 km/h
answerBSource: Cycles may travel alongside one another
answerCSource: Cycle traffic may not be impeded

questionTextSource: Who may enter a street that has this sign?
explanationSource: Vehicular traffic other than cyclists may not use cycle lanes unless permitted by additional sign. The additional sign allows residents to enter the bicycle lane.
answerASource: All cyclists
answerBSource: All persons visiting residents
answerCSource: All moped riders

questionTextSource: This road traffic sign is displayed for your lane. What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates the prescribed minimum speed. You must therefore drive at least 60 km/h, provided that the road and traffic conditions allow it.
answerASource: I must drive at a minimum speed of 60 km/h, if permitted by the road and traffic conditions
answerBSource: I may only use this lane if my vehicle can and is allowed to drive at a speed of least 60 km/h
answerCSource: I may not drive faster than 60 km/h

questionTextSource: What situation applies according to this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The additional sign limits the speed limit between 10 pm and 6 am.
answerASource: - applies between 10 pm and 6 am
answerBSource: - applies between 6 am and 10 pm
answerCSource: - does not apply to electric vehicles

questionTextSource: What should you do when you see this road sign?
explanationSource:
The arrow on this traffic sign always shows the direction of travel. If it is a straight arrow, you have to turn in front of the sign. If it is a curved arrow, you must turn according to the sign. 
You have to flash to indicate the change in driving direction.
answerASource: - indicate left
answerBSource: - turn left before the traffic sign
answerCSource: - turn left behind the traffic sign

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign grants you priority in a specific place. Thus, the right of way for the driver is limited and has no general validity.
answerASource: - only at the next crossroads or junction
answerBSource: - at all crossroads and junctions on this road
answerCSource: - as far as the urban limit

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates that the motorway starts here. This also gives effect to all the rules that apply to this particular type of road.
answerASource: The start of an autobahn
answerBSource: The end of an autobahn
answerCSource: The start of a clearway for motor vehicles only

questionTextSource: What do the white markings on the road mean in this situation?
explanationSource: At intersections and junctions with “right before left” regulation, especially in confusing places, are also used by waiting lines. Wartelinia are often used that look as similar to many small way to go-along.
answerASource: - highlight a duty to give-way due to the right-before-left rule
answerBSource: - indicate a traffic-calming zone
answerCSource: - are a warning about the poor state of the road surface

questionTextSource: What is the rule created by the road markings in this situation?
explanationSource:
You have to nourish yourself this intersection carefully, as it applies right to the left.
At intersections and junctions with “right before left” regulation, especially in confusing places, are also used by waiting lines. Wartelinia are often used that look as similar to many small way to go-along.
answerASource: - approach the junction cautiously
answerBSource: - give way to the [red vehicle]
answerCSource: - come to a stop in front of the road markings

questionTextSource: Which traffic sign gives right of way at the next crossroads?
explanationSource: Only the first traffic sign gives you right of way at the next intersection. The second traffic sign, however, indicates the end of the priority road and traffic sign number three regulates the right of way at a bottleneck.
answerASource: Traffic sign 1
answerBSource: Traffic sign 2
answerCSource: Traffic sign 3

questionTextSource: Which traffic sign gives right of way?
explanationSource: The correct traffic sign to observe the right of way is number two. Number one indicates that a dangerous turn is in front of the driver while number three indicates the number of a national highway.
answerASource: Traffic sign 2
answerBSource: Traffic sign 1
answerCSource: Traffic sign 3

questionTextSource: What is allowed on priority roads outside built-up areas?
explanationSource: On priority roads outside of built-up areas, you may: 1. wait at the right side of the road; 2. park on a hard shoulder. On the other hand, parking on the road is prohibited. Parking vehicles constitute a hindrance and/or danger to flowing traffic, since vehicles are traveling much faster here than within the closed local area. Since a hard shoulder is not considered a lane, you may use it for parking. If there is a possibility to dodge on a hard shoulder, you should also use this for waiting shortly.
answerASource: Stopping on the right edge of the roadway
answerBSource: Parking on the hard shoulder
answerCSource: Parking on the roadway

questionTextSource: What ends here?
explanationSource: This traffic sign announces the change of a priority rule. It marks the end of the previous priority route. The signs "Give way" and "Stop! Give way!" that you can find on intersections and T-junctions, have a similar meaning.
answerASource: A priority road
answerBSource: A controlled parking zone
answerCSource: All previous zonal prohibitions

questionTextSource: What do you do when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The traffic sign gives you priority over oncoming traffic. The oncoming traffic is obliged to wait in front of the obstacle. Nevertheless, you must prepare for stopping when oncoming traffic is already in the bottleneck. You have to let those drivers drive out. You may proceed with right of way if the bottleneck is free.
answerASource: You may only take precedence when the narrowing is clear
answerBSource: You must be ready to stop despite taking precedence
answerCSource: You must give precedence to the oncoming traffic

questionTextSource: What do these traffic signs allow?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates a parking lot that may only be used by residents with a correspondingly numbered parking pass. Other road users are not allowed to park there and have to switch to other parking places.
answerASource: Residents holding a parking licence with corresponding number may park here
answerBSource: All residents may use any of these parking spaces
answerCSource: Everybody may use this parking space as long as other parking spaces are still free

questionTextSource: Are you allowed to park here?
explanationSource: There are special parking spaces for people with impaired mobility or the blind. An official parking permit identifies the vehicles of these persons or their escorts. Even short parking on these areas is forbidden to other drivers.
answerASource: Yes, as a seriously disabled person with an official parking permit
answerBSource: Yes, as a person accompanying blind persons with an official parking permit
answerCSource: Yes, briefly for shopping

questionTextSource: What must you observe when parking here?
explanationSource: In this parking lot you are only allowed to park with a valid ticket. Attach the parking ticket legibly behind the front or rear window of your car. Be sure not to exceed the parking time stated on the ticket. The parking disc does not replace the parking ticket: the parking disc is only valid if the parking meter is out of operation.
answerASource: The parking time indicated on the parking ticket may not be exceeded
answerBSource: The parking ticket must be put in a place so it can be easily read when controlled
answerCSource: Parking ticket and parking disc rank equal

questionTextSource: Which vehicles are allowed to park here?
explanationSource: The traffic sign regulates the parking situation for the road user. The sign allows parking partly on the sidewalk, the side strip and on the green verge for vehicles up to a total weight of 2.8 tons.
answerASource: Vehicles with a permissible total mass of up to 2.8 t
answerBSource: Motorcycles
answerCSource: Vehicles with a permissible total mass of more than 3.5 t

questionTextSource: What must a motor vehicle's maximum speed, determined by its construction, be at least if you want to use this road?
explanationSource: You must be able to achieve at least a maximum speed of 60 km/h on a motor road with your vehicle. The beginning of a motor road is always marked by a traffic sign.
answerASource: 61 km/h
answerBSource: 51 km/h
answerCSource: 81 km/h

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This is a dead end. You can drive normally in a dead end, but the road ends so you might have trouble to turn your car around to leave the street again.
answerASource: A cul-de-sac
answerBSource: An underpass
answerCSource: A road closed to vehicle traffic

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign shows that a first aid station such as "Red Cross" or "Samaritan" is present at this point. However, it does not indicate any danger zone.
answerASource: A first aid station
answerBSource: A crossroads
answerCSource: A danger spot

questionTextSource: What must you remember when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: If you see this sign at lanterns, then you have to secure your vehicle with your parking light. Since these lanterns are not lit up all night, it's very unsafe to leave an unlit car here.
answerASource: Street lighting does not stay on all night
answerBSource: Vehicles may be parked here all night without lights
answerCSource: Vehicles may not be parked here

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign refers to a main road. The number stands for the number of the respective federal highway and not for example for a speed limit.
answerASource: A federal road (Bundesstraße)
answerBSource: Vehicles may not drive faster than 35 km/h
answerCSource: Vehicles must drive at least 35 km/h

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The diversion arrow points out to you as a car driver that you are on a traffic jam-prone section of the motorway and points out an alternative route. But you are not obliged to follow this.
answerASource: An autobahn section which is normally not used very much
answerBSource: The place where the autobahn must be exited
answerCSource: An obligatory diversion for vehicles with dangerous goods

questionTextSource: During daylight, you enter a lit tunnel marked like this. What do you do?
explanationSource: As the car driver, you must turn on the low beam. The daytime lights are not enough, because the light conditions in the tunnel cannot be compared to daylight. You are obliged to make your vehicle seen.
answerASource: I switch on the dipped headlights
answerBSource: I do not have to switch on the lights
answerCSource: I switch on the side lights

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign shows you an emergency lay-by. There you can and should wait if you have a breakdown. It's possible that there's already a vehicle there. For breaks an emergency lay-by may not be used.
answerASource: There may be a vehicle in the next emergency bay
answerBSource: In case of breakdown, you should stop in the emergency bay indicated
answerCSource: You may stop here to have a break

questionTextSource: What do you have to do when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign is located in front of the entrance to a tunnel. Turn on the low beam and remove sunglasses for unrestricted visibility.
answerASource: Take off your sunglasses
answerBSource: Switch on dipped headlights
answerCSource: Switch on the hazard warning lights

questionTextSource: You drive into a brightly lit tunnel which is indicated like this. What do you do?
explanationSource: Even a brightly lit tunnel cannot reach daylight conditions, so you need to drive in with low beam to make your vehicle recognizable. The daytime running light is simply not enough in this case.
answerASource: Drive with dipped headlights
answerBSource: Drive without lights
answerCSource: Drive with daytime running lights

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign combination indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign points to the end of the traffic-calming zone. Now higher speed rules apply again. As a car driver, you have no right of way when you leave and must be careful when entering.
answerASource: - a traffic-calming zone
answerBSource: - a pedestrian zone
answerCSource: - a children's playground

questionTextSource: What does this traffic road sign mean?
explanationSource: You drive on a priority road. The traffic sign is repeated at each intersection or T-junction where you have priority. Outside built-up areas, parking on priority roads is prohibited. On these roads people drive relatively fast and parking vehicles would be a hindrance or a threat.
answerASource: Priority road
answerBSource: No parking on the carriageway outside of settlements
answerCSource: Expressway

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates a numbered detour. Since there are several detours, they are numbered. So, you know exactly where to drive.
answerASource: A numbered diversion
answerBSource: Tram line no. 3
answerCSource: A numbered underground car park

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of when you see this traffic sign combination?
explanationSource: The priority road is turning due to the additional sign. It's a turning priority road. If you stay on the priority road, you will need to indicate that. You have to take care of pedestrians crossing the street, if necessary, you have to stop. The answer: "Driving to the left is mandatory" is wrong, since this traffic sign does not dictate a direction of travel.
answerASource: If I am following the priority road, I have to signal a left-turn
answerBSource: If I continue driving straight ahead, I have to signal a right-turn
answerCSource: The direction of traffic to the left is prescribed

questionTextSource: What is the correct action on roads with these types of protective cycle lanes?

questionTextSource: What can red-white warning plates on vehicles indicate?

questionTextSource: What must you remember in the interests of environmental protection?
explanationSource: In the interests of environmental protection, you as a car driver must pay attention to the following: 1. Avoid driving unnecessarily; 2. Operate the engine evenly at medium speed; 3. Shut off the engine during extended periods of idling - after about a minute. In general, running the engine at idle will result in faster engine wear and wastes energy. You should therefore warm the engine in medium speed range and possibly stay in this area. High engine speeds lead to increased fuel consumption and therefore pollute the environment.

answerBSource: To avoid driving back and forth unnecessarily
answerCSource: To drive with high revolutions per minute too often

questionTextSource: What must be regularly serviced to prevent high fuel consumption and excessive pollutant emission?
explanationSource: The fuel consumption of a vehicle should be as low as possible. As a car driver, you should therefore regularly check and maintain all the components of your vehicle that are important for fuel consumption. These are, for example, the ignition and injection system and the engine air filter and the carburetor.

answerBSource: Carburettor or fuel-injection system
answerCSource: Ignition system

questionTextSource: What happens as a result of tyre pressures being well below the recommended levels?

The effort required to keep the tire running is significantly higher than when the tire pressure has been set correctly. This leads to higher wear on the tire, increased fuel consumption and harms the environment.

questionTextSource: When should you switch off the engine to save fuel and reduce pollution?

These include, for example, moments in traffic jams or in front of level crossings. Although there is an increased consumption during starting, it is compensated again starting from a downtime of one minute. You should not turn off the engine at a stop sign.

answerBSource: When you have to wait at level crossings or building sites

questionTextSource: How can you reduce the noise made by a car?

A driving style predominantly in the low-speed range saves fuel, is environmentally friendly and quiet. The minimum speeds must, however, be met, otherwise the engine runs twitchy, is cogging and sputtering. The lower the speed at which the engine is still running without cogging, the lower the noise.

answerBSource: By using all-terrain tyres

questionTextSource: Where can used oil, lubricants and car batteries be taken to be disposed of properly?

questionTextSource: How can you drive fuel-efficiently when travelling at a constant speed?
explanationSource: At constant speed, you can save fuel with the following measures: 1. by driving evenly in the low speed range; 2. by using the cruise control. The low speed range is just above the minimum speed that the engine needs for its optimal performance. If you drive below that, the engine is cogging; if you drive over it the fuel consumption increases in the high-speed range due to the engine performance. With steady driving in the low-speed speed range, the engine runs smoothly and with low fuel consumption. Cruise control allows a uniform driving style and relieves the / the car driver.
answerASource: By using cruise control
answerBSource: By driving at a low engine speed
answerCSource: By driving at a high engine speed

questionTextSource: At which engine speed range will fuel consumption be lower?
explanationSource: An engine consumes the least fuel in the speed range just above the minimum speed. This is the so-called low-speed speed range. When driving in the low-speed range, the speed should always be above the idle speed. An engine below the minimum speed runs twitchy, is cogging, sputtering or turns off. As a rule of thumb, the lower the speed at which the engine runs without cogging, the more fuel can be saved.

answerBSource: At high engine speed

A pure gearshift to idle is not recommended, as even at idle the engine does not turn off and continues to use fuel.

questionTextSource: What measures help protect the environment?

Low-friction oils are synthetic oils with improved flow behavior, the so-called viscosity. This lubricates the engine faster and better and reduces friction. This also leads to a noticeable saving of fuel. Only oils approved by the engine/vehicle manufacturer should be used to prevent engine damage.

answerBSource: - low viscosity engine oil
answerCSource: - air-conditioning systems

questionTextSource: In which situations should you switch off the engine to save fuel and reduce environmental pollution?

questionTextSource: How should you change gears in order to drive with particular consideration to the environment and fuel saving?
explanationSource: Changing gears environmentally friendly means to upshift early and shift down late. Do not drive the gears into the upper speeds, because this leads to the highest fuel consumption. You should start in first gear and then switch directly to second gear if possible. However, in some traffic situations, such as on the highway acceleration lane or while overtaking, it is necessary to drive with high speed. Of course you should do this in such a situation.

questionTextSource: What can you do to help protect the environment?
explanationSource: The best way to protect the environment is to drive less. All journeys that are not absolutely necessary should be avoided. Certain driving styles consume extra fuel: Short-distance driving, especially when the engine is still cold, stop-and-go traffic, for example in crowded inner cities and searching for a parking space. On top of that, in areas with heavy traffic, the risk of smog from the exhaust emissions of vehicles increases. Driving at full throttle will not only cause noise, but your fuel consumption will increase disproportionately. However, once you are only 20% below the maximum speed, you reduce your fuel consumption by about 40%. Carpooling is also good for the environment.
answerASource: - driving short distances
answerBSource: - driving at flat-out speed
answerCSource: - driving in crowded city centres

questionTextSource: How can you achieve efficient fuel consumption and low pollutant emissions?

All important information about this topic can be found in the operating instructions of your vehicle. If the tire internal pressure is too high or too low, there will be an increase in pollutant emissions. Even unbalanced driving can lead to high fuel consumption. Therefore, instead of accelerating to the upper rotational speed limit and only change gears then, you should shift up in the lower or middle rotational speed limit range. This means you consume less fuel and at the same time reduce the exhaust emissions of your car.

answerCSource: By using the total capacity of every gear

questionTextSource: What should you do after starting the vehicle?

At too low speeds, the engine is cogging and sputtering. The consumption of an engine is the lowest just above the minimum speed.

questionTextSource: How can you save fuel?
explanationSource: You can save fuel by: 1. driving smoothly and anticipatory while avoiding high engine speeds; 2. remove unused roof structures, as they increase fuel consumption with air resistance; 3. swap winter tires for summer tires in the warm season. Driving smoothly means avoiding unnecessary acceleration and deceleration. For example, by slowly rolling toward a red light, you can use the rest of the momentum to drive on if it is already green when you reach the traffic light. Only having to give gas, saves fuel compared to starting from standing completely still.

answerBSource: - driving in a foresighted manner
answerCSource: - driving with winter tyres the whole year round

questionTextSource: How can you minimise tyre wear?
explanationSource: Driving with winter tires in non-winter weather leads to increased tire wear. Strong acceleration is associated with increased abrasion. The tire pressure recommended by the vehicle manufacturer must be observed. Too little tire pressure increases the tire wear considerably. If the air pressure is too low, the tire will deform more with each rotation than with correct tire pressure. Thereby the fabric layers of the tires rub against each other. Heat can be generated that irreparably damages the tire.

answerBSource: I regularly check the tyre inflation pressure
answerCSource: I use winter tyres all year round

questionTextSource: What can cause skidding on bends?

Too low or too different tire pressure reduces driving stability and increases the risk of skidding on turns. Too high a load has an influence on the driving stability. There is not too little loading.

questionTextSource: What can cause skidding on bends?
explanationSource: In curves, the following factors can lead to skidding: 1. excessive speed; 2. wet or icy roads; 3. strong braking. In turns, much of the stiction forces are needed for the cornering forces that hold the vehicle in the lane. If you brake too much, the stiction is instead used largely for the transmission of the braking force. As a result, the cornering forces are too low to counteract the centrifugal force, the same applies to excessive speed. On wet roads, a film of water between the road surface and the tire reduces grip and promotes skidding.

answerCSource: Changes in the road surfacing

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: The overtaking vehicle will probably not be able to complete its overtaking manoeuvre as planned. To prevent an accident, you should let the overtaking vehicle get into the lane.
answerASource: I reduce my speed so that the overtaking vehicle can pull in ahead of me
answerBSource: I accelerate so that the overtaking vehicle can pull in behind me
answerCSource: I maintain my speed because there is sufficient safety distance

questionTextSource: Why should you allow the truck to change lane?
explanationSource: If you are in the dead corner of the truck and the truck suddenly exhaust yourself, a dangerous situation can occur and you would have to slow down. Since there is enough space between the vehicle in front, you should take the truck into account and enable it to change lanes.
answerASource: Because the driver of the truck may fail to see me
answerBSource: Because I should show due consideration to the truck
answerCSource: Because the alternate merging procedure applies here

questionTextSource: As a motorcyclist , why is it important that you adopt an anticipatory driving style especially on very winding stretches of road?
explanationSource: By riding with foresight, you can recognise the behaviour of oncoming traffic and potentially dangerous situations, such as dirt on the road or a change in cornering radius, in good time and adapt your riding behaviour accordingly.
answerASource: Because the turning radius can reduce as the road continues
answerBSource: Because otherwise I will not be able to see dirt on the roadway in time
answerCSource: Because it means I am able to adjust myself in good time to the actions of oncoming traffic

questionTextSource: As a motorcyclist, you can easily go unnoticed by other vehicle drivers. How can you reduce this risk?
explanationSource:
Try to put yourself in the shoes of the drivers involved so that you can better understand what they might do next. This way, you may be able to recognise a potential hazard and react to it in good time.
If you are driving in a vehicle's side blind spot, the driver will not be able to see you. If possible, drive in such a way that you are not in the blind spot for an unnecessarily long time. You should also try to make eye contact with the drivers involved before turning to ensure that they can see you.
answerASource: I avoid remaining for too long in the blind spot to the side of other vehicles
answerBSource: I try to see the road situation from the perspective of other vehicle drivers
answerCSource: Before taking a turn, I try to establish eye-contact with other drivers in my vicinity

questionTextSource: What can cause a dangerous situation?
explanationSource: If a foot brake has a strong one-sided effect, the vehicle may swing off laterally during braking. The car may skid and overturn. Therefore, unequal braking forces are extremely dangerous. Excessive steering can also lead to skidding at high speeds because you can no longer keep the lane properly. A disturbed traffic radio is not a technically dangerous defect.
answerASource: Brakes which engage heavily on one side
answerBSource: Too much steering-wheel play
answerCSource: Disturbed reception of traffic information on the radio

questionTextSource: What are the effects of listening to loud music - especially with an extremely loud bass volume - in the car?
explanationSource: Loud music in the car leads to two negative effects: The driver is distracted by the music and he cannot concentrate on the road. Deep basses in particular lead to strong uncertainty in blind road users. These can lose their orientation, especially with low-frequency music.
answerASource: Blind people lose their orientation when they are near the car
answerBSource: The driver’s attention is significantly impaired
answerCSource: It makes it easier for blind people to recognise where the road is

questionTextSource: What can create a hazard when driving?
explanationSource: If the dipped headlights are not working, you will see poorly on the one hand and you will be seen worse on the other. If the service brake is too one-sided, the vehicle could swerve or have a longer braking distance. If there is too much play in the steering, the steering command will not be carried out.
answerASource: A service brake that responds heavily on one side
answerBSource: An excessive amount of play in the steering
answerCSource: A faulty dipped headlight

questionTextSource: You are driving on a narrow road. About 20 m ahead of you a child suddenly runs onto the roadway. When is a collision unavoidable despite emergency braking?
explanationSource: For this question, you must first calculate the thinking distance and the hazard braking distance. At 50 km/h, the thinking distance is 50/10 x 3 = 15. So, there are 15 meters of thinking distance. The hazard braking distance is determined by the following calculation: [(50/10 x 50/10) / 2] = 12.5. 50 km/h is the right answer.
answerASource: 50 km/h
answerBSource: 30 km/h
answerCSource: 20 km/h

questionTextSource: Why do you have to brake?
explanationSource: You have to expect the person standing in front of the stationary truck to move to the left. Braking serves to protect the person.
answerASource: Because someone is walking onto the road
answerBSource: Because passengers are exiting the tram
answerCSource: Because the truck is switching into my lane

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: When turning right, you must always be aware of pedestrians and cyclists approaching. They may cross the road in front of you.
answerASource: I stop
answerBSource: I continue driving as before
answerCSource: I accelerate

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource:
The road sign signals increased caution against children who could walk across the road between the vehicles.
In addition, you have to expect that people get off to the side of the road.
answerASource: Children walking out onto the road
answerBSource: People exiting their vehicles via the side nearest the road
answerCSource: There is a pedestrian crossing up ahead

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: Since a person wants to cross the intersection, you have to stop and have the person crossed the traffic light.
answerASource: I stop
answerBSource: I continue driving as before
answerCSource: I accelerate

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when you are driving in the left-hand lane of an autobahn at high speed?
explanationSource: At high speed, motorcycles are very sensitive to wind from the side. Small fast motorcycles are hard to see, other vehicles might therefore not see you.
answerASource: Motorists in front of you may suddenly pull out to overtake
answerBSource: Even a slight, unexpected side wind can effect road stability
answerCSource: If you have full beam switched on, vehicles travelling in front of you will not pull out to overtake

questionTextSource: When is there a danger of a motorcycle veering sideways at high speed?
explanationSource: Sudden side wind increases the risk of a motorcycle to veer sideways. Sudden side wind can occur when exiting under a bridge, out of a tunnel, or when overtaking a truck.
answerASource: When you overtake a truck
answerBSource: When driving under a bridge at high speed in a strong side wind
answerCSource: When the braking effect is different on both sides of the front disc brake

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when it starts raining after a longer period of dry weather?
explanationSource: Rain clean up part of the dirt on the road. If the weather was dry for a long period of time, the road will accumulate a lot of dirt. With the addition of water, it will create a film on the road that will make the surface more slippery and increase the braking distance. You can also expect poor visibility on your helmet or goggles.
answerASource: A dangerous greasy film on the surface of the road
answerBSource: Bad visibility through helmet visor or goggles
answerCSource: Shorter braking distance

questionTextSource: You come unexpectedly upon a stretch of road with loose chippings. What do you do?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Avoid braking if possible
answerBSource: Hold your course if possible
answerCSource: Brake hard using the handbrake

questionTextSource: What can happen if you drive around a left-hand bend near the marked central line?
explanationSource: When turning left, being near the central line is dangerous. Due to the inclination left, you could be hit by a vehicle going in the opposite direction. Road markings such as the central line are slippery, if your motorcycle run on it, it could slip resulting in a fall
answerASource: Because of the inclined position, the upper part of your body could be hit by oncoming traffic
answerBSource: The motorcycle could catch the central line and slip from under you
answerCSource: There is no danger as long as the tyres are new

questionTextSource: What are the dangers on wet cobblestones?
explanationSource: Cobblestones are slippery and will therefore increase the risk of falling or tires to lose adhesion to the road and spin when accelerating as well as a tendency of breaks to lock
answerASource: Increased tendency for the brakes to lock
answerBSource: Increased danger of falling
answerCSource: Spinning and skidding of the rear wheel when accelerating

questionTextSource: What should you particularly remember when it is raining?
explanationSource: Rain reduces visibility. The road surface is less visible when wet. The visibility can also be lowered by water particles coming from other vehicles. Your visor will also accumulate water droplets and further diminish your visibility.
answerASource: Visibility through goggles or helmet visor gets considerably worse
answerBSource: Road surface damage is less noticeable when the road is wet
answerCSource: Visibility is further reduced by water sprayed up by other vehicles

questionTextSource: What should you remember when there are tramlines in your lane?
explanationSource: Tramlines are made of metal which is slippery. A motorcycle therefore has a higher risk of sliding and falling on them. When crossing you should try to be as perpendicular to them as possible to avoid slipping
answerASource: Tramlines should always be crossed at the widest angle possible
answerBSource: There is always greater danger of falling on tramlines
answerCSource: Tramlines should always be crossed at the narrowest angle possible

questionTextSource: What can cause the danger of falling on bends?
explanationSource: When turning on bends, slippery surfaces such as lane markings, oil on the road, dirt, manholes, sand or leaves can lead to a fall
answerASource: Oil on the roadway
answerBSource: Lane markings
answerCSource: The outside edge of the bend is higher than the inside

questionTextSource: What can cause the danger of falling on bends?
explanationSource: When turning on bends or braking, slippery surfaces such as lane markings, oil on the road, dirt, manholes, sand, dust, gravel, wet surfaces, cobblestones or leaves will increase the risk of falling
answerASource: Manhole covers on the roadway
answerBSource: Leaves on the roadway
answerCSource: Sand on the roadway

questionTextSource: When must you apply the brakes with particular care?
explanationSource: Braking is less effective on slippery surfaces (wet road, cobblestones, dirt road) and needs to be applied with care
answerASource: When travelling on cobblestones
answerBSource: When it starts raining
answerCSource: On a concrete roadway with good grip

questionTextSource: What can cause in aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) on wet road surfaces?
explanationSource: Aquaplaning - also known as hydroplaning - occurs when the tread depth of the tires is too low to displace the accumulated amount of water or when the amount of water to be discharged is too large due to a high speed (from 60 km/h). In addition, wheel ruts in the road, in which water accumulates, favor this effect.
answerASource: High speed
answerBSource: Worn tyres
answerCSource: Grooves in the roadway

questionTextSource: Why could overtaking be dangerous here?
explanationSource: Overtaking in slush can lead to many dangers. Under the snow there could be a layer of ice that makes overtaking dangerous. In addition, you could hurl snow masses at other vehicles or to start swerving back and forth yourself.
answerASource: There is still slush on the overtaking lane
answerBSource: Slush could be thrown up and impair visibility
answerCSource: Because beneath the slush there could also be ice on the road

questionTextSource: What must you do if your vehicle is caught by a strong side wind?
explanationSource: If your vehicle is hit by strong crosswinds, you should not accelerate, slow down instead and countersteer. The faster you drive, the less secure you can keep your vehicle on the road, since crosswinds can lead to skidding of the vehicle.
answerASource: Steer against the wind
answerBSource: Reduce speed
answerCSource: Accelerate rapidly

questionTextSource: When can strong side wind be particularly dangerous?
explanationSource: Changing wind directions are dangerous when driving. Sudden gusts can catch your vehicle and push it aside. You have to countersteer strongly to keep your vehicle in the lane. If the wind changes direction, you have to react quickly and adjust your steering behavior. Strong crosswind is a danger especially on bridges and on level, undeveloped and unwooded terrain. If you overtake one or more trucks, you will have to expect changing pressure conditions when entering the slipstream of the truck and when leaving the truck. At forest aisles or when leaving wooded areas, a strong crosswind can also hit you abruptly.
answerASource: When overtaking trucks
answerBSource: When driving across bridges
answerCSource: When driving past firebreaks in forests

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with here?
explanationSource: If you drive on the motorway in a strong crosswind from the right, the crosswind constantly pushes the car to the left. You have to countersteer to the right to stay in the lane. If you overtake a truck, expect that the pressure from the crosswind is gone immediately as you enter the slipstream of the truck. If you continue to countersteer, there is a risk that you drive against the truck. Since the crosswind comes from the right, the front vehicle could be pushed to the left.
answerASource: Your vehicle could be blown to the left by the strong side wind
answerBSource: When overtaking, your vehicle is pulling to the right when you enter the slipstream of the truck
answerCSource: The vehicle travelling in front of you can be blown to the right

questionTextSource: A strong side wind is blowing from the right. When are you particularly exposed to danger when overtaking?
explanationSource: If there is strong crosswind from the right, you are particularly at risk as a car driver if you enter the slipstream of a truck as part of an overtaking maneuver. This danger arises from the fact that strong side wind from the right pushes your vehicle to the left, whereupon you automatically start countersteering. As you overtake a truck, you fall into its slipstream; the crosswind is instantly gone and you have to adjust the steering. As soon as you get out of the slipstream, the pressure starts again with the crosswind; So, you have to countersteer again.
answerASource: When you enter the slipstream of the truck
answerBSource: When you leave the slipstream of the truck
answerCSource: When you change lane after overtaking

questionTextSource: Why must you reduce your speed in heavy rain?
explanationSource: Water on the windshield or visor breaks the light and worsens the view, whirling up water also obstructs the view. The braking distance is longer on wet roads because of lower grip. The profile of the tires drains the water. At high speed, the amount of water to be drained is too large, there is a risk of aquaplaning. In this case, there is no contact between the road and the tire. The vehicle cannot be controlled any longer.
answerASource: Because braking distance is increased
answerBSource: Because visibility is reduced
answerCSource: Because aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) can occur

questionTextSource: What adversely affects road safety here?
explanationSource: The rain and the whirled-up water create a haze that can significantly affect traffic safety. Assume that the grip of the tires is significantly lower due to the wet road surface than on dry roads and that this prolongs your braking distance. The low beam of oncoming traffic does not affect the traffic safety, as the car driver is not dazzled by it.
answerASource: Poor visibility
answerBSource: Reduced grip of the tyres on the wet roadway
answerCSource: The dipped headlights of oncoming traffic

questionTextSource: What can be caused if the road surface suddenly becomes worse?
explanationSource: As the road surface deteriorates, it always results in a change and deterioration of traction. As the wheels lose stiction, the vehicle is slipping and skidding faster. The tire noise also depends on the condition of the road surface and changes when, for example, the road turns from a paved road to a cobblestone track.
answerASource: Danger of sliding and skidding
answerBSource: Changes in tyre noise
answerCSource: Longer reaction distance

questionTextSource: What are the special conditions when driving along tree-lined avenues?
explanationSource: The road is usually very narrow. In addition, it is often heavily curved to drain rainwater. Therefore, special care should be taken with oncoming traffic. Curves are not clear due to the trees. You have to drive particularly careful because of the possible oncoming traffic in curves. Left turns are especially dangerous. The trees don't offer any protection to the driver, not even windbreak.
answerASource: Bends are often very narrow and blind
answerBSource: The narrow and often cambered roadway requires special caution when there is oncoming traffic
answerCSource: The trees provide protection from the sides

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: While overtaking a cyclist, the oncoming truck also partly gets on your lane. Brake and drive as far to the right as possible.
answerASource: I brake
answerBSource: I drive over to the right-side as far as possible
answerCSource: I remain in the centre of my lane

questionTextSource: Why should you brake now?
explanationSource: Snow is a special danger for you as a car driver. Your lane is covered in snow. In addition, your view is restricted by a heap of snow. The upcoming green car does not respect the right of way. Therefore, you should brake early, because due to the slipperiness of the braking distance is extended. Probably the green car is also hampered by the snow pile.
answerASource: - green car
answerBSource: - blue car
answerCSource: - tractor

questionTextSource: Why should you ready yourself to have to stop here?
explanationSource: The street looks very narrow. Expect that you have to stop when the truck approaches you. You should drive as far to the right as possible and prepare for a stop to avoid a collision.
answerASource: Because of the truck
answerBSource: Because of the motorcyclist

questionTextSource: What should you be prepared here?
explanationSource: Dirt on the road can increase (not decrease) the braking distance and could make the road more slippery thus affecting the stability of your vehicles
answerASource: - extend the braking distance
answerBSource: - negatively affect driving stability
answerCSource: - reduce the braking distance

questionTextSource: While driving, you unexpectedly come upon a straight section of road with loose chippings. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: On the straight unexpected section with rolling gravel you should carefully reduce your speed for safety reasons and maintain your driving line as much as possible.
answerASource: I slow down carefully
answerBSource: I keep driving as straight as possible
answerCSource: I use my parking brake to brake sharply

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: Due to the bad weather conditions, you should reduce your speed and avoid jerky steering movements (see danger sign) as you could otherwise skid.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I avoid abrupt manoeuvres
answerCSource: I steer hard to the right

questionTextSource: What can be the effect of aquaplaning?
explanationSource: In the event of aquaplaning, your vehicle has no direct contact with the road surface, so the steering command and the braking command cannot be implemented.
answerASource: Reduced ability to steer the vehicle
answerBSource: Reduced vehicle braking capability
answerCSource: Increased tyre static friction

questionTextSource: A strong side wind is blowing. When is there a risk that your motorcycle will be shifted sideways at high speed?
explanationSource: Wind conditions change when overtaking a truck, when driving up a bridge and when leaving a tunnel. Be prepared for crosswinds.
answerASource: - overtake a truck
answerBSource: - exit a tunnel passage
answerCSource: - cross a bridge

questionTextSource: What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates a risk of skidding and sliding. To avoid skidding, you should reduce your speed, especially when it is raining and leaves are falling on the road, as is the case here. Braking hard, accelerating hard or steering jerkily can cause the tyres to lose their grip and you could skid or slide.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I avoid abrupt steering movements
answerCSource: I brake hard

questionTextSource: What should you be prepared for here?
explanationSource: Because of the rain, you should be prepared for the fact that other vehicles will not be able to see your vehicle and that your braking distance will be longer due to the reduced grip.
answerASource: For an extended braking distance
answerBSource: For the poor visibility of my vehicle
answerCSource: For increased tyre traction

questionTextSource: Where are you particularly at risk during an overtaking manoeuvre here?
explanationSource: If there is a strong side wind from the right, you as a motorist are particularly at risk if, when driving off-road, you overtake in the slipstream of a truck. This hazard arises from the fact that strong cross winds from the right push your vehicle to the left, whereupon you automatically countersteer. As you overtake a truck, you slipstream into it; the crosswind is gone instantly and you have to adjust the steering. As soon as you pull out of the slipstream, the pressure starts up again with the crosswind; You have to countersteer again.
answerASource: When driving into the truck’s wind-sheltered area
answerBSource: When driving out of the truck’s wind-sheltered area
answerCSource: When changing lane after overtaking

questionTextSource: What could happen if you pass through a bend close to the lane markings?
explanationSource: Always make sure that you and your motorbike are not overhanging the centre line. If you take blind bends, you are unnecessarily endangering yourself and oncoming traffic. This is because you could be hit by oncoming traffic or lose control of your motorbike during evasive manoeuvres.
answerASource: - be struck by oncoming traffic
answerBSource: - lose control of my motorbike while attempting to avoid oncoming traffic
answerCSource: - be able to see well around the bend

questionTextSource: What can increase the risk of a motorcycle fall while driving through road bends?
explanationSource:
Oil on the road increases the risk of falling when cornering, as the tyres' grip is significantly reduced due to the slippery surface.
Unevenness in the road surface, such as bumps, reduces the grip of the tyres and therefore increases the risk of falling.
answerASource: An oil spill on the carriageway
answerBSource: A bump on the carriageway
answerCSource: A carriageway inclines towards the inside of the bend

questionTextSource: How can you reduce the risk of falling when encountering wet road markings?
explanationSource:
As the road markings are slippery, especially when wet, you should avoid driving over them if possible. If it is not possible to drive around them, you should take the shortest route across them to minimise the risk of falling.
Due to the wetness, you should avoid accelerating or braking here to minimise the risk of falling.
answerASource: I avoid driving on the road markings as much as possible
answerBSource: I take the short distance possible when driving across the road markings
answerCSource: I brake as sharply as possible before encountering the road markings

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate when driving on wet cobblestones?
explanationSource:
An uneven and wet road surface and therefore less grip increases the risk of the rear wheel spinning or slipping when accelerating.
When braking on wet cobblestones, the tendency to lock up is increased, which can cause the rear wheel to slip sideways more quickly.
Locking tendency means that the front wheel is loaded more and the rear wheel is unloaded when braking.
answerASource: A high tendency for the wheels to lock during braking
answerBSource: A rapid skidding of the rear wheel when accelerating
answerCSource: An increased traction of the front wheel during steering

questionTextSource: What should you take into account when you drive a motorcycle during low ambient temperatures?
explanationSource:
If the temperature of the outside air differs from the air temperature in the helmet, the helmet will mist up. This greatly impairs visibility. 
In addition, the handbrake lever is more difficult to operate with cold fingers.
answerASource: The helmet visor is prone to misting up
answerBSource: The handbrake lever cannot be safely operated with cold fingers
answerCSource: Freezing improves the ability to concentrate

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource:
You can see from the windsock that a strong wind is blowing from the right. The strong wind could move your vehicle to the left. Be prepared to steer to the right.
In addition, when entering the slipstream of the truck, you must be prepared for your vehicle to be moved to the right because its large surface area will keep out the crosswind. Be prepared to steer to the left to avoid colliding with the truck.
answerASource: My vehicle may be forced leftwards
answerBSource: When driving into the wind-protected area alongside the truck, my vehicle may be forced rightwards
answerCSource: The truck ahead of me may be forced rightwards

questionTextSource: Heavy rain has caused large puddles to form on the road surface. What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: You can easily lose control of your motorbike when riding through large puddles. Ride slowly and do not lift your feet to keep your motorbike stable and avoid splashing water.
answerASource: - slowly to avoid create road spray
answerBSource: - quickly to maintain the stability of my motorbike
answerCSource: - with my feet elevated to prevent getting wet

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate while driving, when it starts to rain after a long dry period?
explanationSource: Basically, you must always expect visibility through your helmet visor to be poorer in the rain and, if it is dry for a long time, a layer of dirt will remain on the road which can form a slippery film on the road surface when it rains, so you must expect the road to be particularly slippery.
answerASource: A particularly slippery road surface
answerBSource: Poor visibility through my helmet visor
answerCSource: A shorter braking distance

questionTextSource: Where does aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) occur particularly often?
explanationSource: If the speed is too high and/or there is more water than can be drained from the tire profiles, aquaplaning occurs. A water film prevents contact between tires and the road. The vehicle is no longer controllable. The risk of aquaplaning exists in particular in ruts and road subsidence. Level crossings usually have no increased risk for aquaplaning, since water can usually run well here.
answerASource: In dips in the roadway
answerBSource: Where there are grooves in the roadway
answerCSource: At level crossings

questionTextSource: What does aquaplaning (sliding on wet roads) lead to?
explanationSource: Aquaplaning or hydroplaning can lead to neither being able to brake or steer. As a result, conscious driving of the vehicle is no longer possible. It can get into a skid. This effect is caused by a kind of water gore, which slides between the tire and the road.
answerASource: The vehicle can no longer be steered and braked
answerBSource: The vehicle can leave the road
answerCSource: Steering becomes heavy

questionTextSource: What must you reckon with when it starts raining after a long period of dry weather?
explanationSource: If rain sets in after prolonged drought, a dangerous film of lubricant forms on the road, which is only washed away after a long period of rain. This film is more dangerous than rain alone. Brake very carefully. This lubricating film is also deposited on the windscreen. After turning on the windscreen wipers, it first worsens the view drastically. The braking distance is extended by the poorer traction.
answerASource: A dangerous greasy film on the surface of the road
answerBSource: Bad visibility immediately upon switching the windscreen wipers on
answerCSource: Shorter braking distance

questionTextSource: It starts raining. Why must you immediately increase the safe distance?
explanationSource: When it starts raining, you must immediately increase the safety distance for the following reasons: Under the influence of moisture, the dirt, dust and fine deposits from tire abrasion or pollen on the road turn into a dangerous film of grease. This reduces the traction and thereby extends the braking distance. This lubricating film also forms on the windscreen and on the brake drums. If you use the windscreen wipers, they will first worsen your view and smudge the window. On the brake drums, this lubricating film leads to a slower response of the brakes in wet conditions.
answerASource: Because a greasy film may form which increases the braking distance
answerBSource: Because visibility may become worse and the windscreen wipers do not immediately provide a clear field of vision
answerCSource: Because the brakes react more quickly

questionTextSource: You are driving in dense fog on a federal road (Bundesstraße) outside a built-up area. Are you allowed to drive in the middle of the road continuously straddling the broken white line?
explanationSource: Driving on the center line is not allowed even in dense fog, especially as drivers can always collide with oncoming traffic due to reduced visibility. If there's fog, slow down and orient yourself on the right side of the road for a safe ride.
answerASource: No, because it could represent a danger to oncoming traffic
answerBSource: Yes, if fog headlamps and rear fog lamps are switched on
answerCSource: Yes, because it reduces the risk of running off the road

questionTextSource: What must you do on encountering sudden dense fog driving at daytime?
explanationSource: As a car driver you must turn on low beam during the day when there's fog, snowfall and other poor visibility. Because of the insufficient luminosity driving with parking light is not permitted. Adjust your speed to the visibility conditions and use the rear fog light when you can see less than 50 meters.
answerASource: Adapt speed immediately to visibility conditions
answerBSource: Switch on dipped headlights
answerCSource: Only switch on parking lights

questionTextSource: How can you achieve sufficient visibility through the windscreen?
explanationSource: Turn your windscreen wipers to interval switching, during fog in order to remove fine water droplets from the mist. Turn on your windscreen wipers before overtaking or being overtaken so that obstructions caused by slush or spray water are immediately removed. Avoid fogged windows with the correct ventilation setting: Set ventilation to outside air supply and not to circulating air. Open the windows a gap to let the moisture escape. Briefly switching on the air conditioning dehumidifies the air very quickly.
answerASource: Switch on the windscreen wipers from time to time when it is foggy
answerBSource: When there is slush, switch on the windscreen wipers already before slush is thrown onto your windscreen by other vehicles
answerCSource: When the windscreen is steamed up, switch the ventilator off and keep the windows closed

questionTextSource: You want to overtake on a narrow avenue not within a built-up area. What must you be aware of in particular?
explanationSource: Due to the narrow width of the lanes, even cyclists cannot be overtaken with oncoming traffic. Often, the road is heavily curved to drain rainwater, that means when crossing the center line, the vehicle tilts to the other side. This load change reduces the driving stability. Due to the trees, curves can be confusing. Vehicles with tall bodies often need to travel in the center to avoid colliding with branches and could reach into your lane.
answerASource: Even cyclists frequently cannot be overtaken when there is oncoming traffic due to the narrow width of the roadway
answerBSource: Misjudgements of distances and oncoming traffic frequently result in serious accidents
answerCSource: Vehicles with high superstructures frequently move to avoid branches

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of in this situation?
explanationSource: A sudden and strong change of lighting conditions, you must reduce your speed, because the human eye otherwise cannot react fast enough to the change of light. Other road users or obstacles are very difficult to perceive in dark areas.
answerASource: The speed should be adjusted due to the visibility
answerBSource: Judging distances is made more difficult because of the constant changing of light and shadows
answerCSource: The constant changing of light and shadows makes it more difficult to detect other road users in good time

questionTextSource: When overtaking several trucks, you are faced with this situation. What should you do now?
explanationSource: The visibility is very poor due to the weather and the road is not sufficiently visible. In order not to endanger anyone, you should stop the overtaking process as quickly as possible and ensure that you are at a safe distance from the vehicle in front of you when you go back into lane.
answerASource: I abort the overtaking manoeuvre
answerBSource: After pulling back into lane, I again establish an adequate safety distance
answerCSource: I continue overtaking quickly and pull back into lane in front of the red truck

questionTextSource: You are driving as part of a group of several motorcycles. What reduces the risk of accidents?
explanationSource: When driving together with a group of motorcycles, all should keep their distance behind each other and have enough distance to brake and stop
answerASource: - maintain their position
answerBSource: - assume smaller safety distances
answerCSource: - drive alongside one another in the same lane

questionTextSource: When is there an increased risk of falling during steering and braking manoeuvres?
explanationSource: When turning on bends or braking, slippery surfaces such as lane markings, oil on the road, dirt, manholes, sand, dust, gravel, wet surfaces, cobblestones or leaves will increase the risk of falling
answerASource: On wet cobblestone pavement
