explanationSource: No traffic jam can be seen in this situation. Since the motorcycle is driving on the left, it will not leave the motorway. On the other hand, the car driving on your left flashes to the right. You can be prepared for the driver to leave the motorway.
answerASource: [The yellow car] exits the motorway
answerBSource: The motorbike exits the motorway
answerCSource: The [blue bus] in front of me brakes due to a traffic jam

questionTextSource: You want to exit the motorway. What is creating a dangerous situation here?
explanationSource: The motorbike behind you (see right wing mirror) creates a dangerous situation for you. Take this into account when leaving the motorway.
answerASource: The motorbike
answerBSource: [The yellow truck]
answerCSource: [The green vehicle]

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: There is an object on the roadway in the right lane in front of the truck. Truck will avoid the object by using the left lane. You should therefore stay in the left lane. If you continue to drive unchanged, you could collide with the truck. You should therefore reduce your speed.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I remain in the left-hand lane
answerCSource: I continue driving as before

questionTextSource: Why must you reduce your speed now?
explanationSource: The right lane is blocked in front of the truck. He has to change to the left lane. To allow him to do this, you should reduce your speed. The car driving behind you is no reason for you to slow down. The maximum permissible speed here is 120 km/h. Keep this in mind.
answerASource: Because of the truck
answerBSource: Because of the speed limit
answerCSource: Because of the [blue] car

questionTextSource: You want to leave the motorway. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: As soon as you have entered the deceleration lane, you should brake. If you brake before entering the deceleration lane, you will unnecessarily interfere with traffic on the motorway. You should check your speedometer more often to make sure you are not entering the bend too fast. On motorways, there is a danger that you will get too used to the high speed and therefore enter an exit too fast. If you were to slow down at the turn, you would not be able to reduce your speed in time. In general, you should always brake before a bend and not only in the bend.
answerASource: I use the speedometer to check my speed at short intervals
answerBSource: I reduce my speed now
answerCSource: I reduce my speed only once I am at the turn

questionTextSource: When driving on motorways, why should you maintain the recommended speed of 130 km/h?
explanationSource: The higher the speed, the longer the reaction distance, braking distance, etc. If all vehicles travel at a similar speed, the traffic flow becomes more even.
answerASource: - can prevent accidents occurring
answerBSource: - can reduce the severity of possible accidents
answerCSource: - achieves an even flow of traffic

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate here?
explanationSource: Due to the sudden traffic jam, the driver of the truck sees the end of the traffic jam too late. Due to the traffic jam, the motorcycle will try to go through the rows of vehicles.
answerASource: The driver of the [yellow truck] does not notice the start of the tailback in time
answerBSource: The motorbike drives between the lines of vehicles
answerCSource: [The blue car] pulls into my lane in front of me

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate when driving out the exit slip road?
explanationSource: A warning triangle indicates a nearby obstacle, e.g. people, a defective vehicle or a fallen load, in the exit lane. Therefore, enter the exit at a very reduced speed.
answerASource: A broken-down vehicle
answerBSource: People on the carriageway
answerCSource: A fallen load

questionTextSource: Why should you avoid overtaking alongside the roadworks site?
explanationSource:
When overtaking in the construction site area, the risk of accidents is significantly increased, because there is a risk that the vehicles that you want to overtake run over the yellow lane marking. The lanes are mostly narrower and the distractions on the edge of the road can lead to inattentiveness.
Overtaking in the construction site area often saves almost no time, since most drivers in the construction site area only drive the maximum speed.
answerASource: The time saved is frequently insignificant
answerBSource: Vehicles in the right-hand lane may cross over the yellow road markings
answerCSource: The risk of an accident increases significantly

questionTextSource: How can you reduce the risk of driving onto a motorway against the stipulated direction?
explanationSource: To avoid driving against the direction of travel on the motorway, you should always pay attention to the relevant traffic signs. It is very important to pay attention to the exact signs, especially when the traffic routing has changed.
answerASource: I deliberately look for traffic signs showing the right direction of travel at motorway slip roads
answerBSource: I pay close attention to the signage when the traffic routing changes
answerCSource: When encountering roadworks, I only follow the information provided by my navigation system

questionTextSource: What must you remember as bus driver on duty?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: - not have any beverages containing alcohol while on duty and while on stand-by duty
answerBSource: - not start to drive while under the effect of alcohol
answerCSource: - have beverages containing alcohol while on duty, as long as a blood alcohol standard of 0.3 per mille is not exceeded

questionTextSource: What must you remember as bus driver on duty?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: You may not take any drugs or medicaments which impair your ability to drive while on duty or stand-by duty
answerBSource: You may not start to drive when your ability to drive is impaired by drugs or medicaments
answerCSource: You may take any drug for which no prescription is required, without having to fear that your ability to drive is impaired

questionTextSource: What must you do if you notice signs of fatigue when driving on open stretches of road at night (heavy eyelids, feeling chilly, sudden frights etc.)?
explanationSource: You can only avoid signs of fatigue if you take sufficiently long breaks in time. Do not turn on the radio to stay awake, as there is a risk it will distract you further. If you already feel the first symptoms of fatigue, find a place where you can take a longer break as soon as possible.
answerASource: Interrupt your journey for an adequate break
answerBSource: Switch on the radio and proceed

questionTextSource: How can fatigue be prevented on a long journey?
explanationSource: Timely breaks prevent overfatigue. Gymnastic exercises during the break pep up the circulation and are good for the joints. Coffee has only a short-term mobilizing effect, which can then quickly turn into the opposite. That means you could fall asleep without warning.
answerASource: By well-timed and sufficient stops for rests
answerBSource: Gymnastic exercises during stops
answerCSource: Drink coffee and make no stops

questionTextSource: What is the potential risk when using driver assistance systems?
explanationSource: The car driver may have too high expectations of the advanced driver-assistance system. This may result in the car driver neglecting his or her own responsibility and paying too little attention to the traffic. Even when using advanced driver-assistance systems, the responsibility for safe driving remains with the driver.
answerASource: - have false expectations of the systems
answerBSource: - pay too little attention to the traffic around him
answerCSource: - neglect his own personal responsibility

questionTextSource: What is the risk if you become distracted while using electronic devices when driving?
explanationSource: Using electronic devices while driving can cause you to miss traffic signs and wander off your lane (concentration distracted).
answerASource: I may deviate from my lane
answerBSource: I may fail to notice road traffic signs
answerCSource: My stopping distance may be reduced

questionTextSource: What frequently causes younger drivers to have “disco accidents”?
explanationSource: All three are correct. The so-called disco accidents are caused by overtiredness, by physical exhaustion and by being unfit to drive.
answerASource: - are over-tired
answerBSource: - are physically exhausted
answerCSource: - are not fit to drive

questionTextSource: What is the right thing to do if you sense signs of fatigue during a night-time journey?
explanationSource: As soon as you feel tired, you should take a break immediately. The risk of falling asleep increases with every second. If possible, you can let your passenger drive if they have the driving licence.
answerASource: I interrupt the journey to take an adequate break
answerBSource: I allow a co-driver to drive, if possible
answerCSource: I decide against taking a break, and fight the tiredness

questionTextSource: What can diminish alertness in road traffic?
explanationSource: All external stimuli such as joy, anger, interesting things on the radio and lively conversations can negatively affect the attention while driving, as they can be distracting.
answerASource: Strong emotions (e.g. delight, annoyance, impatience)
answerBSource: Animated conversation
answerCSource: Engrossing programs on the car radio

questionTextSource: What strongly distracts you while you are driving?
explanationSource: Telephoning while driving is only allowed via a hands-free system. Even then it is still a distraction. The lighting of a cigarette is considered a strong distraction, as it directs the view from the roadway to the cigarette. Whining children and passengers can be very distracting, if necessary, take a pause.
answerASource: Telephoning
answerBSource: Lighting a cigarette
answerCSource: Nagging children on the backseats

questionTextSource: When should you interrupt your journey?
explanationSource:
Especially on longer trips, it can always happen that concentration subsides. If you notice that your concentration wanes noticeably, you should definitely take a break.
You should also stop if you want to cancel an object that has fallen into the vehicle. By searching and lifting an object, you can distract yourself very much. Therefore, you can endanger yourself and other road users.
answerASource: - want to pick an item that has fallen down in my vehicle
answerBSource: - notice that the concentration is decreasing
answerCSource: - want to activate the cruise control system

questionTextSource: A slow driver prevents you from proceeding quickly. What is right?
explanationSource: Never start a risky overtaking maneuver or force your frontman to speed up with your flash light. Keep the necessary safety distance and wait for a convenient opportunity for safe overtaking.
answerASource: You follow him until there is a possibility to overtake
answerBSource: You flash your headlights to make him drive faster
answerCSource: You close up to him in order to shorten the overtaking distance

questionTextSource: What makes a good driver?
explanationSource: A good driver adheres to the traffic regulations and is characterized by a cooperative behavior in dealing with other road users. He/she refrains from risky driving styles and careless overtaking maneuvers. A good driver does not have to prove his/her skills to others.
answerASource: He treats others like partners, drives calmly and cooly and refrains from showing that he is superior
answerBSource: He uses every possibility to overtake, give the slip to others and show his superiority even if his manner of driving involves risks
answerCSource: He drives fast in order to show his abilities

questionTextSource: Travelling on a main road after a lengthy period of time of following behind, you are finally able to overtake a very slow-moving car. What should you do?
explanationSource: You should stay calm in this situation and overtake with a sufficient safety distance without endangering other road users. Harassing others by tightly cutting them should be avoided. Otherwise an accident could occur.
answerASource: - I pull back in after an adequate distance in order not to impede the other driver
answerBSource: - I brake briefly in order to call the attention of the other driver to his slow speed
answerCSource: - I drive especially slowly in order to emphasise to the other driver the effects of his driving style

questionTextSource: The vehicle ahead has prevented you from overtaking for some time now. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: The car driver has to wait until overtaking is possible without danger and always maintain the necessary safety distance to the vehicle in front. Stay calm, because tailgating, turning the headlights on full beam for a long time or honking is considered coercion and will be punished with fines or even a withdrawal of your driving license.
answerASource: I wait until I am able to overtake
answerBSource: I close the gap
answerCSource: I sound my horn and flash my headlights persistently

questionTextSource: Right before you want to drive off, you got annoyed about something and have become very angry. What should you do?
explanationSource: Getting into the car angrily can negatively affect your driving style. If you are angry, your driving style is also aggressive and limited. Therefore, only get into your vehicle when you're abreacted.
answerASource: I only drive off, once I have calmed down again
answerBSource: I drive off, in order to distract myself from my anger
answerCSource: I try to reach my destination quickly, so that I can calm down there

questionTextSource: You have just avoided a road traffic accident by performing an emergency braking manoeuvre. Your hands and knees are shaking. You are still far from your destination. What should you do?
explanationSource: You should calm down first. Because your restlessness can have a negative effect on your driving style. Find a place to stop and relax. Afterwards you may continue the journey.
answerASource: - to the next stopping place, in order to calm myself down
answerBSource: - to my destination, so that I can recover my composure

questionTextSource: What should you be aware of when driving a car after a long day's work?
explanationSource: After a long day at work, you are usually not fully productive and get tired faster. That's why mistakes while driving can increase and your concentration decreases.
answerASource: I drive with a lower level of concentration
answerBSource: I will become tired more quickly
answerCSource: I make fewer driving errors

questionTextSource: How can you recognise a good driver?
explanationSource: A good driver always takes care to act in partnership and also knows that people can make mistakes.
answerASource: - exhibits a calm and relaxed driving style
answerBSource: - refrains from responding to provocations on the road
answerCSource: - always informs other traffic participants of their driving errors

questionTextSource: The [red] car in front of you has been driving slowly for some time now. What is the correct course of action in this situation?
explanationSource:
You maintain your speed so as not to tailgate and keep the required safe distance. It is forbidden to tailgate, tailgate too closely or honk your horn in traffic.
The road is clear, but you can only see as far as the next bend. The visible distance is too short for an overtaking manoeuvre.
answerASource: I maintain my speed
answerBSource: I overtake, because the road is clear
answerCSource: I close the distance and sound my horn

questionTextSource: What frequently causes young people to have “disco accidents”?
explanationSource:
Young drivers in particular have less driving experience and a higher willingness to take risks through showing off. Dangerous situations are assessed more poorly and accidents occur more frequently.
A defensive driving style can prevent accidents.
answerASource: A lack of awareness of the road traffic
answerBSource: A high readiness to take risks
answerCSource: A defensive driving style

questionTextSource: Why can driving under pressure of time be dangerous?
explanationSource: When you are under time pressure, you may underestimate dangers because your willingness to take risks increases and people often drive too fast. You should still drive defensively and stick to the speed limits to avoid endangering yourself and others.
answerASource: - risks are frequently underestimated
answerBSource: - the driver frequently goes too fast
answerCSource: - a defensive driving style is frequently adopted

questionTextSource: You are on a road outside of a built-up area, and a car in front of you is driving much slower than is permitted to and has the capacity to. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: Even if you don't like it, you must adapt your speed to that of the car and not get too close to it. Only overtake at a suitable place and not in front of it. Do not endanger yourself or others.
answerASource: I adjust my speed to that of the car
answerBSource: I overtake at a suitable place
answerCSource: I flash my headlights, until the car begins to drive faster

questionTextSource: Why is it not advisable to change lanes frequently?
explanationSource: Every lane change requires certain activities: observing traffic, flashing, changing the lane yourself. In this case, other road users can be overlooked and endangered. Changing lanes often doesn't save you much time, but the more often you change lanes, the more likely you are to make mistakes.
answerASource: Because this can increase the risk of driving errors
answerBSource: Because the time gained by this is generally very small
answerCSource: Because this can endanger other road users

questionTextSource: You have narrowly avoided a road traffic accident through emergency braking. Your hands and knees are shaking. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource:
Stop at the next opportunity and take a break until you have calmed down. Maybe you can get out, walk a few metres and take a deep breath. 
You should also go through the situation again in your head later, as you may realise what didn't go perfectly. That way you can adjust your driving behaviour in the future.
answerASource: I take a break at the soonest possible opportunity, in order to calm myself
answerBSource: Later, I ponder if I could have avoided the situation
answerCSource: I continue driving in order to calm myself

questionTextSource: While driving, you become very annoyed at the behaviour of another road user. What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: It is important that you are not distracted by the traffic if you are annoyed by the behaviour of other drivers, because road safety is paramount. Concentrate on the traffic and continue driving calmly; if you are emotionally agitated while driving, you could overlook dangers. If necessary, take a break and only continue driving when you have calmed down.
answerASource: I continue to concentrate on the road traffic
answerBSource: I continue driving in such a way that my anger does not negatively affect road safety
answerCSource: If necessary, I take a break to calm myself down

questionTextSource: Why does environment friendly driving in general have a beneficial effect on traffic safety?
explanationSource: Avoid high speeds and drive anticipatory. This not only has a positive effect on the environment. It also contributes to greater road safety.
answerASource: Because drivers think ahead when travelling, so that possible dangers can be recognized in time and well avoided
answerBSource: Because environment friendly driving avoids high speeds so that dangerous situations occur less frequently
answerCSource: Because you can only drive slowly using alternative fuels

questionTextSource: The queue you are driving in is overtaken in an inconsiderate manner by a car. Because of oncoming traffic the driver wants to join the queue in front of the car driving in front of you. This driver is annoyed and "closes the gap". What do you do?
explanationSource: In such a situation, by deliberately giving in, you should reduce the speed and giving the other road user space to go back into lane in order to reduce the risk of an accident. Remember that everyone can misjudge.
answerASource: You give in and let the jostling driver join the queue
answerBSource: You also "close the gap" in order to teach the jostling driver a lesson
answerCSource: Nothing, since you are not concerned

questionTextSource: A truck indicating to the right is driving in front of you. An impatient car driver is jostling you. What can you do?
explanationSource: In this situation it is better to cut back and keep a safe distance. Maybe the truck driver would like to signal that the road is clear. However, it may also be that he/she intends to stop or turn right. Only overtake if there is no danger. Increase the distance to the truck so that the pushy driver can overtake.
answerASource: I only overtake when I am completely certain that the traffic situation allows overtaking without danger
answerBSource: I open up the distance to the truck to make overtaking easier for the jostling driver
answerCSource: I overtake because I assume that the truck driver wishes to signal to me that overtaking is possible

questionTextSource: On a road not within a built-up area, a car is driving in front of you at a speed somewhat slower than that permitted. You are annoyed at the slower-moving car. What should you do?
explanationSource: You should definitely wait for a safe situation to overtake. Harassing someone by tailgating or flash light, however, represents a threat or even coercion. You should refrain from doing so.
answerASource: I adjust my speed to that of the vehicle ahead
answerBSource: I overtake at a suitable place
answerCSource: I flash my headlights, until the car in front begins to drive faster

questionTextSource: What can frequently lead to road traffic accidents during night-time recreational drives at the weekend?
explanationSource: Too little driving experience during the dark and a lack of knowledge about driving behavior in fully occupied vehicles often leads to accidents during nocturnal recreational rides on the weekend.
answerASource: - little experience driving after dark
answerBSource: - little experience with fully occupied vehicles
answerCSource: - little knowledge of road traffic regulations

questionTextSource: You are under time pressure. What must you ensure?
explanationSource: Even if you are under time pressure, you must follow the traffic rules. This also means that you comply with the speed limits. After all, excessive speed is THE main cause of accidents.
answerASource: - to exceed the speed
answerBSource: - to drive through the traffic lights even when red
answerCSource: - to reduce the safety

questionTextSource: Serious accidents frequently occur when young people drive cars during night-time recreational drives at weekends. What causes can be particularly relevant here?

answerBSource: High risk readiness
answerCSource: Hesitant driving style

questionTextSource: After overtaking you, a car pulls back into lane without leaving sufficient space in front of you. What should you do?
explanationSource: The car driver in the overtaking vehicle shows a misconduct that should not disturb you. Stay calm, do not honk and do not turn on the flash light. Decelerate slightly and restore the required safety distance between the vehicles.

questionTextSource: The risk of causing an accident when driving a vehicle is significantly higher for drivers aged between 18 and 24. What reasons are frequently relevant here?
explanationSource: Young car drivers often have too little experience to be able to correctly assess road traffic risks. They often drive carelessly and riskily, overestimate their abilities and rely on the safety of the vehicles they control. By testing their car and their own driving skills, they sometimes get into unmanageable situations.

answerBSource: Over-estimation of own capabilities
answerCSource: Higher levels of risk readiness

questionTextSource: While you are in a traffic jam, looking in your rear-view mirror you see a motorcyclist "snaking" past the stationary line of vehicles. What should you do?

answerCSource: I take steps to ensure that he too must come to a stop

questionTextSource: You are driving on a main road and a car following behind is "tailgating" you. How should you react?
explanationSource: When driving on a highway and being harassed by the car behind you, you should keep calm and control your speed at an appropriate pace. You should avoid slow driving so as not to hinder the flow of traffic. However, you should also not be tempted to drive faster. It's best to let the pushy driver pass at the next opportunity.

questionTextSource: You overtake on a motorway. Behind you a car approaches at high speed, with headlights and indicators flashing. What should you do?

If you have sufficient overtaking speed, you can still complete the overtaking process. As soon as the next opportunity arises, you should switch to the right lane and let the pushy driver pass.

answerCSource: I gently decelerate

questionTextSource: Five young friends are driving in a car to a recreational event. The mood is exuberant. To what risks can this give rise?
explanationSource: For the car driver, there is a risk of getting carried away by the mood. He/She can be distracted by the others and overlook traffic hazards and make driving mistakes. It is recommended to set up rules of conduct for the passengers before starting the journey. So, the car driver is not a spoilsport or tempted in any way.

answerCSource: - be induced to drive slowly

questionTextSource: While driving on the motorway you overtake several vehicles. A vehicle approaches you from behind with indicators and headlights flashing. What should you do?

For this reason, you should try to change to the right-hand lane in compliance with the safety distance. Any braking maneuver would only worsen this dangerous situation. Switching on the hazard light is prohibited in this case.

answerCSource: I brake briefly to prompt the vehicle behind to keep its distance

questionTextSource: You were in a bad mood prior to commencing your journey. What consequences should you avoid when driving?

questionTextSource: What indications mean you should interrupt your journey?

questionTextSource: What is the rule of thumb when calculating the braking distance for evasive braking on a dry, even and asphalted roadway?
explanationSource: The rule of thumb for determining the stopping distance is: thinking distance (speed divided by 10 times 3) plus braking distance (speed divided by 10 times speed divided by 10). At 50 km/h, this is 50 divided by 10 times 3 equals 15 m thinking distance plus 50 divided by 10 times 50 divided by 10 equals 25 m braking distance. The stopping distance is 40 m. In case of emergency braking, this value is halved. That would be 20 m in this example.
answerASource: ({Speed in km/h}/{10} x {Speed in km/h}/{10}) : 2
answerBSource: ({Speed in km/h}/{10} x 5) : 2
answerCSource: ({Speed in km/h}/{10} x 3) : 2

questionTextSource: You are travelling at 40 km/h. The braking distance for evasive braking is approximately 8 metres. How long is the braking distance at 50 km/h given otherwise the same conditions?
explanationSource: The normal braking distance of your car is determined by this rule of thumb: Speed ​​divided by 10 times speed divided by 10. To calculate the braking distance during emergency braking, the determined value is halved again (divided by 2). Here there means: 50/10 * 50/10 = 25, divided by 2 = 12.5 meters.

questionTextSource: You are driving at 100 km/h on a road not inside a built-up area. In front of you a truck is travelling at 70 km/h. What is the minimum distance you must be from a crest in the road when commencing an overtaking manoeuvre?
explanationSource: The rule of thumb for the overtaking path is calculated from the speeds, the overtaking time and the vehicle lengths. The formula is s = v1 / (v1−v2) * (l1+l2+sv+sh). V1/ (V1-V2)) times L. V1 =100 km/h, V2 = 70 km/h, L = 2 times 50 m safety distance plus the vehicle lengths. Truck 12.5 m plus car 4.5 m = 17 m. L = 117 m. 100 km/h divided by 30 km/h times 117 m = 389.9 m, that means 400 m overtaking path. In confusing areas this has to be doubled. You must be at least 800 meters from the crest of the road.

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What should you do now?
explanationSource: You must wait with overtaking until the driver driving in front of you has finished overtaking. A solid line and the no overtaking sign indicate a restriction on overtaking. A broken line (guideline), however, means that overtaking is not prohibited.
answerASource: I do not overtake yet, because the [blue car] could overtake
answerBSource: I follow right behind the [blue car] if it overtakes
answerCSource: I overtake all the vehicles in one overtaking manoeuvre

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: Plan your overtaking maneuver in a way so that it is completed before the solid line begins. A solid line as well as the corresponding traffic sign indicate that you are not allowed to overtake here.
answerASource: - do not overtake the tram
answerBSource: - overtake the tram on the right
answerCSource: - overtake the tram on the left

questionTextSource: You want to overtake a truck with a trailer. What must you be aware of when doing so?
explanationSource: If you want to overtake a truck with a trailer, you should take into account the overtaking distance required because of the trailer (it is longer) and that the vehicle outline of the truck might obscure vehicles ahead.
answerASource: The necessary overtaking distance is significantly increased due to the trailer
answerBSource: The external bodywork of the truck-trailer combination may conceal vehicles ahead
answerCSource: You may only pull out to overtake once you are directly behind the truck-trailer combination

questionTextSource: Why are you not permitted to now overtake the bus driving ahead of you?
explanationSource: The bus has turned on the warning lights and is still driving to the bus stop. In this case, it is not allowed to overtake the bus. In addition, the motorbike behind you has started to overtake.
answerASource: Because the hazard lights of the bus are switched on
answerBSource: Because of the motorbike
answerCSource: Because of the lack of road markings

questionTextSource: You want to overtake the vehicle driving up ahead of you. What is necessary to allow you to do so?
explanationSource:
Show other road users in good time that you will move onto the left or medium lane. Each overtaking must be displayed by flashing. After you have observed the rear and side traffic and could make sure that the left lane is free, the indicator must be pressed. Then you can change the lane and overtake the slower vehicle at a much increased speed.
Just make sure that you do not exceed the permissible maximum speed.
answerASource: - considerably increase my speed
answerBSource: - use my indicator to signal my intention to overtake
answerCSource: - observe the traffic to the rear and side of me

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The tractor seems to want to turn onto the dirt track on the right. Stay behind the tractor and wait until it has turned off. Overtaking it is dangerous because the implement installed at the rear could swerve.
answerASource: I remain behind the tractor
answerBSource: I anticipate that the tractor may turn off the road
answerCSource: I comply with the tractor driver’s indicator-based prompt to overtake

questionTextSource: You want to overtake [the blue car]. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: Wait for the bike to leave the blind spot. Note that the car driver who is overtaking has to drive at a higher speed than the driver who is being overtaken. Accelerate if necessary and finish your overtaking process before the restriction on overtaking takes effect, such as a solid line.
answerASource: - overtake at a substantially higher speed
answerBSource: - overtake while maintaining the speed driven
answerCSource: - do not overtake, because a motorbike is in moving in the blind spot

questionTextSource: What could make this situation become hazardous?
explanationSource: The car driver approaching from behind tries to overtake you before the turn, although he/she cannot see the curve. If traffic is approaching in this situation, it can lead to dangerous evasive maneuvers by the oncoming traffic and the driver who is overtaking. The tractor next to the road is no danger.
answerASource: Oncoming traffic
answerBSource: The [red car]
answerCSource: The tractor

questionTextSource: You are driving on the motorway in a motor vehicle with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 7.5 t. The heavy snowfall has reduced visibility to less than 50 m. What must you do?
explanationSource: With your vehicle that exceeds a maximum permissible gross weight of 7.5 t, you are not allowed on the leftmost lane driving, because this is there for overtaking for faster cars. You can not with your vehicle faster than 50 km / h drive. You can continue to drive on the highway even with snowfall.
answerASource: I may not use the extreme left-hand lane
answerBSource: I may not drive faster than 50 km/h
answerCSource: I must leave the motorway at the next exit

questionTextSource: You are driving outside built-up areas. Visibility is reduced to just about 50 m due to heavy rain. What applies in this situation?
explanationSource: Here, too (50 m due to heavy rain) applies in reduced visibility is the speed limit 50 km / h. Motor vehicles above 7.5 t street legal. Total mass is prohibited overtaking. The rear fog light does not have to be switched on.
answerASource: Overtaking is prohibited for motor vehicles with a permissible total mass exceeding 7.5 t
answerBSource: The maximum permissible speed is 50 km/h
answerCSource: The rear fog lamp must be switched on

questionTextSource: You are driving an agricultural machine. A queue of cars has formed behind you. What is correct?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Reduce speed at an appropriate place or wait in order to let the cars following you overtake
answerBSource: Switch on the hazard warning lights to show that overtaking is not possible
answerCSource: Agricultural machines are not required to let faster vehicles overtake

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: In this situation, you should pull into the bus stop and let the car behind overtake you
answerASource: I pull into the bus stop on the right, and stop there
answerBSource: I increase my speed
answerCSource: I maintain my speed and continue driving

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: The bottleneck is in your lane. Therefore, you must stop before the bottleneck and wait until the oncoming car has passed.
answerASource: I stop and wait ahead of the constricted point until the oncoming car has passed through
answerBSource: I pass through the constricted point by carefully driving between the oncoming car and the construction site barrier
answerCSource: I signal to the driver of the oncoming car that he should drive closer to the edge of the roadway

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action here?
explanationSource: The distance to the bicycle is not enough. Stop and wait until the biker has passed the bottleneck. This is the only way to be sure that you do not endanger the biker.
answerASource: I slow down and wait
answerBSource: I queue behind the vehicles already waiting on the right
answerCSource: I continue driving at a reduced speed

questionTextSource: You are approaching a "red" traffic light. Several cars are waiting in all lanes. What do you do?
explanationSource: You should always wait in line behind the last car in your lane and not try to pass over other cars by either side.
answerASource: Wait in your lane behind the last car
answerBSource: Pass the waiting cars on the right-hand side and proceed to the stop line
answerCSource: Pass the waiting cars on the left-hand side and proceed to the stop line

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource: In Germany, you must drive on the right. As there is no vehicle in front of you that is travelling slower than you, you must move into the right lane. As soon as slower vehicles appear, you may move back into the centre or left lane to overtake them.
answerASource: I move into the right-hand lane
answerBSource: I stay in the centre lane
answerCSource: I move into the left-hand lane

questionTextSource: How could a dangerous situation arise?
explanationSource: The fast driving style of the yellow truck suggests that he will overtake the blue truck in front of him as well. Their speed is faster than that of the trucks. You cannot dodge to the left as a car on the left lane is directly behind you. If the yellow truck swings out, you have to brake hard.
answerASource: - yellow truck
answerBSource: - green truck
answerCSource: - the car up ahead

questionTextSource: What should you do now?
explanationSource: Since the driver of the car on the right of the acceleration lane increases its speed, you should enable him/her to merge into traffic. Refrain from your right of way and reduce your own speed. You cannot change on the left lane as a vehicle approaches there.
answerASource: I let the blue car merge in front of me
answerBSource: I accelerate
answerCSource: I immediately change from the left lane to the central lane

questionTextSource: What should you do?
explanationSource: Stay on the middle lane and delay. The leading car will also delay soon to let the car on the left lane merge into traffic, because this lane ends. You cannot go to the left, changing to the right lane would be too dangerous as well.
answerASource: I stay in the middle lane and slow down
answerBSource: I move to the right-hand lane and slow down
answerCSource: I move into the left-hand lane and overtake the red car in front of me

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: You drive on the middle lane and in this situation you have to stay on the lane and reduce the speed. The driver on the left lane drives more slowly. You must not overtake him from the right. You cannot change to the right lane, there is a vehicle.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I continue driving without changing speed
answerCSource: I move into the right-hand lane

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate in this situation?
explanationSource: Since the vehicle drives very fast from the right onto the freeway, you have to expect that it will change to the middle lane. This impression is reinforced by the fact that trucks maintain their speed and distance.
answerASource: - grey car will move into the middle lane
answerBSource: - yellow truck will pull out in front of me to overtake
answerCSource: - grey car will remain in the right-hand lane

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: A car drives in front of you and from the right a car drives onto the main carriageway of the freeway. It is clear that the car driving on the left lane has a slower pace than you. Since it's only allowed to overtake on the left, you must delay and reduce your speed.
answerASource: I reduce my speed and stay in the middle lane
answerBSource: I maintain my speed and stay in the middle lane
answerCSource: I maintain my speed and move into the right-hand lane

questionTextSource: You are driving on a road that has several marked lanes going in one direction. When should the alternate merging procedure be employed?
explanationSource: The zipper merge is to be used when a lane cannot be used any further. The exception is on the motorway.
answerASource: When a lane comes to an end
answerBSource: If one lane is not continuously free to traffic due to an obstacle
answerCSource: When a merging lane ends on the motorway

questionTextSource: What is true about using the hard shoulder in this situation?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates that the hard shoulder is to be used like the right-hand lane.
answerASource: - must be used like a right-hand lane
answerBSource: - may only be used in a breakdown situation
answerCSource: - may only be used by trucks

questionTextSource: What is true in this situation about using the hard shoulder?
explanationSource: The hard shoulder may not be used regularly. You can only use it in case of a breakdown.
answerASource: – may only be used in the case of a breakdown
answerBSource: – may only be used by trucks
answerCSource: – must be used in the same way as a right-hand lane

questionTextSource: What is the right behaviour now?
explanationSource: If one of the lanes ends on a multi-lane road, vehicles must be allowed to move to the adjacent lane. Here it is important to drive with foresight in order to be able to react to lane changes in a timely manner and to adjust your own speed if necessary.
answerASource: I reduce my speed
answerBSource: I let the [blue] car in front of me move over
answerCSource: I accelerate

questionTextSource: What applies to this road that is outside of a built-up area?
explanationSource:
This traffic sign draws you aware that you will have to choose a direction shortly. At the same time, you will receive an idea of ​​the intersection, especially the turning traces.
Therefore, you can only use the left lanes outside if you want to turn left.
answerASource: - to perform a left turn
answerBSource: - to overtake
answerCSource: - if there is a traffic jam

questionTextSource: Where are U-turns prohibited?
explanationSource: You may not turn your vehicle on freeways or motorways. Reverse driving is strictly prohibited on both types of roads as well.
answerASource: On motorways
answerBSource: On clearways for motor vehicles only
answerCSource: On farm or forest tracks

questionTextSource: Why do you have to wait before the left turn?
explanationSource: As a left-turning driver, you generally have to give priority to oncoming traffic. Cyclists also have this right of way. So, in this situation you have to let the motorcyclist pass before turning. He will pass the left-turning driver from the right and continue straight ahead.
answerASource: - motorbike
answerBSource: - car
answerCSource: - bicycle

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: If you want to turn left and a car wants to overtake you from the left, you have to let it pass first. Only after the car has completed its overtaking maneuver, you may turn yourself.
answerASource: - once the [red] vehicle has overtaken me
answerBSource: - now in order to make the carriageway clear
answerCSource: - now so that following vehicles will not be confused by my slowing down

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource: Since you are currently being overtaken, it is not possible to turn left. You must wait for the overtaking process to be finished before you can turn left.
answerASource: I stop at the farm track and wait
answerBSource: I accelerate and swiftly take the turn
answerCSource: I continue driving, because I cannot turn off the road here

questionTextSource: In this situation, why do you have to wait before the left turn?
explanationSource: In this situation, you need to wait for the motorbike behind the car to pass before turning left
answerASource: - motorbike
answerBSource: - [red car]
answerCSource: - [bicycle]

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: In this situation, you should first wait for the motorcycle to overtake you before turning left.
answerASource: - after [the red car] has overtaken me
answerBSource: - now, so as not to impede the truck behind me
answerCSource: - now, so that my hesitation does not create any uncertainty in the minds of those behind me

questionTextSource: Why are you not allowed to turn off the road now?
explanationSource: The red car is overtaking and is travelling to your left. To avoid a collision, do not turn off now under any circumstances. Check whether your indicators are dirty and therefore cannot be seen.
answerASource: - [red car]
answerBSource: - bus
answerCSource: - oncoming traffic

questionTextSource: You want to turn left at the next junction. What is the right course of action now?
explanationSource:
If you drive past the waiting vehicles, you could obstruct oncoming vehicles and, in the worst case, block the entire road.
Therefore, start to flash left now to signal to other road users that you want to turn into the road. Also, stay behind the waiting vehicles and only turn off when you reach the road.
answerASource: I remain behind the stationary vehicles
answerBSource: I indicate left
answerCSource: I drive carefully to the left past the stationary vehicles

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. Which line should you steer?
explanationSource: Keep to the centre line when turning left. This allows you to drive as far to the left as possible without driving in the opposite lane and the motorbike behind you can pass you on the right.
answerASource: The middle driving line
answerBSource: The left driving line
answerCSource: The right driving line

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. Which line should you follow?
explanationSource: You should keep as far to the left as possible when turning left. However, you are riding in a one-way street where cycling is permitted in both directions. You are therefore not allowed to ride on the far left because cyclists may be coming towards you. Therefore, follow the centre line.
answerASource: The middle driving line
answerBSource: The left driving line
answerCSource: The right driving line

questionTextSource: You want to turn left. Which line should you follow?
explanationSource: If you want to turn left in a one-way street, you should keep as far to the left as possible, so follow the left lane.
answerASource: The left driving line
answerBSource: The middle driving line
answerCSource: The right driving line

questionTextSource: What is the right course of action when reversing?
explanationSource: When reversing, look to the rear and additionally secure the traffic area by looking all around, drive attentively and slowly and, for safety reasons, make sure that there are no persons in the blind spot.
answerASource: - ensure the road area is safe by additionally looking all around me
answerBSource: - drive slowly and attentively
answerCSource: - make sure that no-one is located in my blind spot

questionTextSource: You are driving a motor vehicle with a gross vehicle weight rating in excess of 3.5 t. At what speed do you have to turn right within a built-up area?
explanationSource: Drive at walking speed to avoid danger and accidents.
answerASource: - walking speed
answerBSource: - at 20 km/h maximum
answerCSource: - at 30 km/h maximum

questionTextSource: Your vehicle is equipped with a rear view camera. You want to reverse into a forecourt from a very busy road. What must you be aware at this time?
answerASource: I should additionally arrange a look-out to help guide me in
answerBSource: I must also observe the traffic directly
answerCSource: I can rely solely on the rear view camera

questionTextSource: What can reduce the risk of serious accidents when turning right within a built-up area?
answerASource: By equipping vehicles with turn assist systems
answerBSource: By driving at walking speed when turning right
answerCSource: By taking rights turns quickly so as not to impede other road users

questionTextSource: You want to reverse your vehicle out of a property lot onto a road. What must you do?
explanationSource: To drive backwards from a property to a road, it is not enough just to use the rear view camera. You should be instructed on the street if necessary and keep in mind that pedestrians have priority.
answerASource: - arrange for someone to direct me, if necessary
answerBSource: - allow pedestrians right of way
answerCSource: - only use the rear view camera

questionTextSource: When turning right, there is a risk of overlooking cyclists travelling straight ahead in the blind spot. How can you reduce this risk?
explanationSource:
When a bus or truck turns right, there is a risk of overlooking cyclists driving straight ahead in the blind spot. To minimize the risk, you should observe the following and side traffic at short intervals, make sure that your mirrors are adjusted correctly and ensure that no accessories are obstructing your view.
This way you can recognize cyclists in good time and avoid accidents.
answerASource: I monitor the traffic behind and to the side at short intervals
answerBSource: I make sure the mirrors are adjusted correctly
answerCSource: I make sure that accessories in the cab do not obstruct the view

questionTextSource: You have mounted a plough which extends far beyond the back of your tractor. What must you remember when you turn right?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Turn only when there is no danger for oncoming traffic caused by the plough moving over
answerBSource: No-one can be endangered when turning if the plough is marked by warning panels

questionTextSource: You have mounted a plough which extends far beyond the back of your tractor. Who can be endangered when you turn right?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Vehicles travelling behind you wishing to overtake
answerBSource: Vehicles in the lane on your left
answerCSource: Vehicles parking on the right-hand side

questionTextSource: To your tractor you have attached a hay turning machine that projects a long way to the back. Who could be endangered by this when you take a left turn?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Vehicles that have pulled in alongside me to my right
answerBSource: Pedestrians on the right-hand side of the road
answerCSource: Vehicles that have pulled in behind me

questionTextSource: You are driving an agricultural tractor with two trailers and you want to take a right turn. What must you look out for?
explanationSource: Since the room increases your agricultural tractor with two trailers when turning to the right, please make sure that the supporters do not compromise when turning people on the edge of the road or on the sidewalk, and if there are right next to you cyclists that are hidden by the blind spot ,
answerASource: Whether there are any cyclists alongside me to the right
answerBSource: That during the turn the trailers will not endanger any pedestrians on the side of the road or on the footpath
answerCSource: That I remember to switch on my hazard lights if I have to swing out far to the left when taking the turn

questionTextSource: You are returning on a tractor from working in a field and want to turn onto a road. What should you observe in order to avoid dangerous situations?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: Keep windscreen clean
answerBSource: Avoid places where drivers with the right of way cannot be seen from an adequate distance
answerCSource: Be directed onto the road if my vision is restricted

questionTextSource: Where is stopping prohibited?
explanationSource: In general, the road traffic act prohibits stopping at the traffic areas when stopping is dangerous for other road users or endangers public safety and order. This is especially true for holding on the lanes of the freeway or on a highway. The same goes for sharp and confusing curves.
answerASource: Outside parking areas on autobahns and clearways for motor vehicles only
answerBSource: Near or on sharp bends
answerCSource: At bus stops

questionTextSource: Where is parking prohibited?
explanationSource: A parking ban in accordance with the road traffic act applies in all places where other road users can be obstructed or the public order and safety is no longer guaranteed by parked cars. For example, parking is prohibited at taxi stands, narrow lanes or entrances and exits in front of property as well as on and up to 5 m before pedestrian crossings.
answerASource: On narrow roadways opposite driveway entrances and exits
answerBSource: At taxi ranks
answerCSource: Immediately behind pedestrian crossings

questionTextSource: Who has priority at this parking space?
explanationSource: The vehicle that reaches the parking space first always has priority. Priority will also be given in the event that the vehicle drives past the parking space just in order to park backwards or perform other movements that are useful for parking in this gap. Priority is also maintained when the vehicle is waiting at the parking space.
answerASource: The white vehicle, because it has arrived at the parking space first
answerBSource: No one, both drivers should agree
answerCSource: Me, because I am parking by driving in forwards

questionTextSource: What do you have to consider when parking your motorbike?
explanationSource: The motorbike must not obstruct or endanger anyone. Make sure that the motorbike is parked safely and in a space-saving manner.
answerASource: - may not obstruct anyone
answerBSource: - may not fall over
answerCSource: - must be positioned on the sidewalk

questionTextSource: When are you allowed to park your two-wheeler on the footpath?
explanationSource: Basically, the lane is to be used for parking a vehicle. Motorbikes may be parked anywhere where car parking is permitted. In general, parking on sidewalks for motorcycles is not permitted unless a traffic sign permits it.
answerASource: When a traffic sign indicates this is permitted
answerBSource: If the footpath is at least 2.0 m wide
answerCSource: If there are no other options

questionTextSource: What should you take into account when parking a two-wheeler?
explanationSource:
When you park your motorbike or scooter, the same rules and prohibitions apply to you as for car drivers. This means that you are not allowed to simply park your motorbike or scooter on the pavement. You must park your bike on the road, just like car owners. Parking on the pavement is also only permitted for motorbikes or scooters in marked parking spaces or if appropriate traffic signs permit this.
You should also always pay attention to the surface on which you park your motorbike; if the surface is too soft or too uneven, your motorbike can quickly fall over and cause other hazards.
answerASource: I should not park the two-wheeler on soft underground
answerBSource: I may park the two-wheeler on footpaths only if this is appropriately signed
answerCSource: When parking the two-wheeler I am released from the duty to display a parking disc

questionTextSource: Both cars want to park here. Which car takes precedence?
explanationSource: In this case, the red car has right of way. He has reached the parking space first. Priority will also be given in the event that the vehicle drives past the parking space just in order to park backwards or perform other movements that are useful for parking in this gap.
answerASource: The red car
answerBSource: The white car

questionTextSource: Where are you allowed to park a trailer with a permissible total mass exceeding 2 t in built-up areas regularly on sundays and public holidays and between 10 p.m. and 6 a.m.?
explanationSource: Parking a trailer with a maximum authorized mass of more than 2 t is permitted in commercial and residential areas with appropriately marked parking spaces. Within closed areas and in special areas for recreational purposes, regular parking is prohibited from 22:00 to 6:00 and on Sundays as well as on public holidays.
answerASource: In industrial areas
answerBSource: In wholly residential areas, on specially designated parking spaces
answerCSource: In special areas reserved for recreation purposes, on sufficiently wide roads

questionTextSource: What must you remember when parking a trailer without a truck?
explanationSource: Please note that you may not leave your vehicle trailers on regular parking spaces for more than two weeks if they are not attached to a towing vehicle. Incidentally, the same also applies to hard shoulders. Accordingly marked parking lots are excluded from this regulation.
answerASource: - may not be parked for more than 2 weeks on public roads
answerBSource: - may be parked for more than 2 weeks on specially designated parking spaces
answerCSource: - may be parked for an unlimited period of time on adequate hard shoulders

questionTextSource: You want to park on the right-hand edge of the road. What should you do in this situation?
explanationSource: You signalled first and therefore have priority to the parking space. To avoid a conflict, you should still give up the parking space.
answerASource: I forego the parking space, even though I have priority
answerBSource: I park now, because I have priority

questionTextSource: You want to park on the right-hand edge of the road. Who has priority in this situation?
explanationSource: The car that stopped in front of you signaled first and therefore had priority in the parking lot. If you park now, there could be a collision.
answerASource: The [blue] car has priority
answerBSource: I have priority
answerCSource: No one has priority. We have to agree

questionTextSource: You want to reverse park on the right-hand side of the road. What is correct in this situation?
explanationSource:
When parking, you always have to pay attention to the oncoming and subsequent traffic, as you could leave your lane when you steer and thereby endanger other road users.
Also make sure that no other vehicle wants to leave its parking space to avoid accidents.
answerASource: I closely observe the oncoming traffic before manoeuvring into the space
answerBSource: I anticipate that vehicles may exit their parking spaces
answerCSource: I switch on my hazard lights

questionTextSource: Where are you allowed to park motor vehicles with a permissible total mass exceeding 7.5 t in built-up areas regularly on sundays and public holidays and between 10 p.m. and 6 a.m.?
explanationSource: In a residential area you would at these times not to win a lot of friends. (Volume) Please parke in industrial areas or designated parking spaces in the spa and clinic areas.
answerASource: In health resorts and hospital areas, on specially designated parking spaces
answerBSource: In industrial areas
answerCSource: In wholly residential areas, on sufficiently wide roads

questionTextSource: Where are you allowed to park regular buses in built-up areas regularly on sundays and public holidays and between 10 p.m. and 6 a.m.?
explanationSource: Coming soon...
answerASource: In industrial areas
answerBSource: At any bus stop
answerCSource: In wholly residential areas

questionTextSource: Before which sign must you maintain a distance of at least 10 m, if it would otherwise be concealed by your vehicle?
explanationSource: In front of signs such as the diagonal crosses, light signals or the sign "Stop! Yield to cross traffic", you at least have to keep a distance of 10 meters. This is especially necessary if they are going to be covered by your vehicle. For example, you are allowed to come to a stop in front of a stop sign. Behind it, however, stopping is prohibited.
answerASource: Diagonal cross
answerBSource: Stop. Give way.
answerCSource: No stopping

questionTextSource: Where must you use a parking disc for parking?
explanationSource: Wherever traffic signs dictate, the car driver must use a parking disc. It is also used when a defective parking meter makes the withdrawal of a valid parking ticket impossible. The use of a parking disc for a limited stopping restriction does not entitle you to park here for more than three minutes.
answerASource: At a defective parking meter
answerBSource: Where stipulated by traffic signs
answerCSource: In a no-waiting area

questionTextSource: The time on a parking meter has not yet run out. What do you do?
explanationSource: If a parking meter has not yet expired, you may use the rest of the parking time and leave your vehicle for free at the parking lot for the remaining time. You do not need a parking disc for this.
answerASource: - use the time left on the meter without inserting more money
answerBSource: - not use the time left on the meter
answerCSource: - only use the time left on the meter when using a parking disc

questionTextSource: At 10.40, you enter an area where parking is allowed for 2 hours when using a parking disc. What time must you set on the parking disc?
explanationSource: The pointer of the disc should be adjusted to the stroke of half an hour following the time of parking. If you arrive at 10.40 hours, the pointer should be at 11.00. Thereby the parking duration does not matter.

questionTextSource: You want to park in a car park where there is a parking-ticket machine. What must you remember?
explanationSource: The car driver must put the valid parking ticket from in the vehicle so that it is visible from the outside. Avoid exceeding the parking time printed on the receipt, also referred to as a parking ticket. A parking disc as a replacement for a parking ticket is only valid if the parking meter is defective.
answerASource: Place a valid parking ticket in your vehicle so it is easy to read from outside
answerBSource: Do not exceed the parking time indicated on the parking ticket
answerCSource: A parking ticket is never needed when using a parking disc

questionTextSource: When may you remain parked at a parking meter without activating it?
explanationSource: After field work, the lighting and signaling device must be cleaned if necessary. The parking time doesn't need to be monitored while entering or exiting and loading or unloading. When driving slowly up to the priority road, the road must be sufficiently visible.
answerASource: To allow people to get in and out of the vehicle
answerBSource: To load and unload the vehicle
answerCSource: To do some quick shopping

questionTextSource: How must you safeguard your motorcycle against unauthorized use?
explanationSource: To avoid other people to use your motorcycle, remove the ignition key and lock the steering.
answerASource: By removing the ignition key
answerBSource: By locking the steering
answerCSource: By engaging top gear

questionTextSource: You want to park your motorcycle on its prop stand. What do you have to consider?
explanationSource: To avoid your motorcycle to tip over, make sure the surface is firm and can support the weight of the motorcycle and that you swing the side stand all the way up to the stop.
answerASource: The surface under the prop stand must be firm
answerBSource: You must extend the prop stand to the end position
answerCSource: You should only park your motorcycle on an upward slope

questionTextSource: You want to park your motorcycle on its main stand. What do you have to consider?
explanationSource: To avoid your motorcycle to tip over, make sure the surface is firm and can support the weight of the motorcycle. You can park your bike that way for a short or long time.
answerASource: The surface under the main stand must be firm
answerBSource: You may only use the main stand to park your motorcycle for a short time

questionTextSource: Where should you not park a motorcycle (using main or side stand)?
explanationSource: To avoid your motorcycle to tip over, make sure the surface is firm and can support the weight of the motorcycle. Also make sure that the surface is not too steep downwards which would make the main or side stand unsafe and at risk of tipping over
answerASource: On soft ground
answerBSource: Where the parking surface is a steep downward slope
answerCSource: On firm ground

questionTextSource: Which precautionary measures must you take when parking a motorcycle?
explanationSource: You must make sure that no other people can use your motorcycle by removing the ignition key and locking in the handlebar/steering. Also make sure that the groud is firm to avoid the motorcycle to tip over.
answerASource: Park on firm ground
answerBSource: Remove the ignition key
answerCSource: Lock the handlebar

questionTextSource: You want to park your motorcycle. What must you consider?
explanationSource: When parking, to avoid your motorcycle to tip over, make sure the surface is firm and can support the weight of the motorcycle.
answerASource: The parking surface must be firm
answerBSource: You can lean the motorcycle against a lamppost if you put it into gear
answerCSource: You must switch on the dipped headlight

questionTextSource: How do you secure your motorcycle against being used without permission?
explanationSource: The steering lock prevents steering in the event of unauthorised access. The engine cannot be started by removing the ignition key.
answerASource: - remove the ignition key
answerBSource: - close the steering lock
answerCSource: - disconnect the battery

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of when using a prop stand?
explanationSource: If the side stand is not fully retracted, it can cause a fall when cornering. If the slope is too great, the motorbike can slide forward because the side stand folds in on its own. If the ground is too soft, the motorbike may sink until it falls.
answerASource: The gradient of the parking place
answerBSource: The characteristics of the ground surface
answerCSource: That the prop stand is completely folded away before driving off

questionTextSource: What should be noted when using the prop stand?
explanationSource:
If the side stand is still folded out and you engage a gear, the side stand switch will automatically switch off the engine. The side stand switch is a safety measure that prevents you from riding off with the side stand folded out.
The side stand is designed for parking a motorbike. Try to avoid slopes when parking. Always make sure that your motorbike is stable and that the side stand is fully extended to avoid danger and damage.
answerASource: Where possible, the motorbike should not be parked on a slope
answerBSource: The prop stand should be fully extended
answerCSource: The prop stand switch can prevent you driving off with the prop stand extended

questionTextSource: How must you safeguard your vehicle against theft?
explanationSource: To secure the car against theft, you must first close all the windows and doors. The ignition key must be removed in any case. Snap the steering wheel lock into place to additionally secure the car.
answerASource: By removing the ignition key, engaging the steering wheel lock and closing the windows
answerBSource: By locking the doors and boot
answerCSource: By activating the differential lock

questionTextSource: What must you do when leaving your car?
explanationSource: Before leaving the vehicle, you have to protect it against rolling away uncontrolled, for example on a downhill slope. Before exiting you have to make sure not to endanger other road users. This also applies to your passengers. Opening doors carelessly when getting out is a common cause of accidents. The vehicle must also be protected against unauthorized use.
answerASource: Before opening the door, look out for traffic behind
answerBSource: Secure the vehicle against rolling away
answerCSource: Secure the vehicle against unauthorized use

questionTextSource: Your car is standing on the edge of the roadway. What should you do when getting into the car?
explanationSource: When entering and exiting, you must take care not to endanger the other road users. The entry from the right - from the sidewalk - is safer, but not without any danger. Before you open the doors, make sure that the traffic situation permits that. Make sure that there are no pedestrians or cyclists on the sidewalk at this time.
answerASource: Let passengers get into the car, if possible, from the pavement
answerBSource: Open the doors on the roadway side only when traffic permits
answerCSource: The doors on the right-hand side can always be opened without danger

questionTextSource: What is important when children are getting in and out of a car?
explanationSource: As a car driver, you should make sure that children get on and off directly on the sidewalk. If the situation does not allow this, they should only get in or out on the side of the road under the supervision of an adult. Children are seen worse and are unpredictable in their behavior.
answerASource: Let the children in or out, if possible, on the pavement side
answerBSource: Let the children in or out on the roadway side only under supervision
answerCSource: Children are not allowed to get in or out of a car on public roads

questionTextSource: What should you call the attention of your passengers to before exiting?
explanationSource: As a car driver you must take the necessary measures to avoid accidents or traffic disruptions when passengers leave the vehicle. Point that out to passengers when they get off on a bike path. Cyclists are not required to wait on bike paths. Anyone entering or leaving the car may not endanger other road users.
answerASource: - they should heed the traffic flowing on the left-side
answerBSource: - they should heed the pedestrians walking on the right-side
answerCSource: - cyclists are required to wait on cycle paths

questionTextSource: Why may you not exit now?
explanationSource: Anyone getting in or out of the car must behave in such a way as to avoid endangering other road users. An approaching cyclist can be seen in your left-hand side mirror, you must let him pass first.
answerASource: Because [of the cyclist]
answerBSource: Because of the motorbike
answerCSource: Because [of the pedestrian]

questionTextSource: You have stopped in a parking strip and now want to exit your vehicle. What must you do in this situation?
explanationSource: Before you open the door and leave the vehicle, check in the left-hand mirror that no one is coming from behind. You must also look to your left to make sure that there are no other vehicles in your blind spot.
answerASource: - by looking in my left-hand exterior rear-view mirror before opening the door
answerBSource: - by glancing over my left shoulder before opening the door
answerCSource: - just by looking through the slightly opened door

questionTextSource: When leaving your car, how do you secure it against unauthorised use?
explanationSource: You have to make sure that no unauthorised persons use your car. Therefore, remove the key from the starter and make sure that all windows and doors are closed.
answerASource: I lock the doors
answerBSource: I close the windows
answerCSource: I activate the child-safety lock

questionTextSource: You want to let a child out of your car. What should you be aware of when doing so?
explanationSource: As a car driver, you should make sure that children get on and off directly on the pavement side. If the situation does not allow this, they should only get in or out of the vehicle on the carriageway side under supervision and with prior warning of possible dangers. Children are harder to see and their behaviour is unpredictable, so you should make sure that they no longer have to cross the road.
answerASource: - exit the car on the same side as the footpath
answerBSource: - not have to cross over the carriageway
answerCSource: - be warned of possible dangers when getting out of the car

questionTextSource: What is allowed when your motorcycle has broken down with engine damage?
explanationSource: You cannot tow a broken motorcycle, it will tip over and fall. Instead you should either push it or transport it.
answerASource: Loading the motorcycle on a vehicle and having it transported
answerBSource: Pushing the motorcycle to the nearest garage
answerCSource: Having the motorcycle towed

questionTextSource: You are outside of a built-up area and have come to a stop due to a fault with your motorcycle. How should it be transported to the garage?
explanationSource:
The operation of a vehicle that is no longer traffic -proof is a significant and, above all, incalculable danger for you and for other road users. Therefore, you have to pull your vehicle out of traffic in the shortest way. You can have your strength bike removed on a suitable trailer or van.
According to § 15a (4) StVO, motorcycles must not be towed with a tow rope.
answerASource: On a suitable trailer
answerBSource: In a suitable van
answerCSource: By using a suitable towrope

questionTextSource: You want to tow another vehicle with your car. What must you know?
explanationSource: You need to know if it is a vehicle with a gearshift, automatic transmission or an electric car. An electric car cannot be towed, it must be loaded. A vehicle with an automatic transmission can only be towed for a few kilometers in level "N" without any damage.
answerASource: You must at least have a driving permit class B
answerBSource: You must at least have a driving permit class BE

questionTextSource: A car with servo brakes has broken down with engine damage. What is correct when towing the car away?
explanationSource: If possible, use a tow bar when towing a vehicle with a brake booster. Because a brake booster works only with the engine running. When the engine is off, only a weak braking effect can be achieved in this vehicle even with strong pressure on the foot brake.
answerASource: The car being towed can only achieve slight braking effect even when increased pressure is applied to the pedal
answerBSource: If possible, use a tow-bar
answerCSource: The action of the foot brake is not impaired by the engine not running

questionTextSource: A car with power steering has broken down with engine damage and must be towed away. What is correct?
explanationSource: The function of the power steering depends on the engine and only works when the engine is running. If the engine and the assistive function of the power steering fail, steering is considerably more difficult and you need more power. The steering is impaired in its function.
answerASource: - is considerably heavier
answerBSource: - is locked
answerCSource: - is not impaired

questionTextSource: At what distance must the warning triangle be placed when a car has broken down on a road with fast-moving traffic?
explanationSource: If you are staying on a road with fast traffic with your vehicle, you must set up the warning triangle at a distance of approx. 100 m. Make the breakdown situation visible to other road users by switching on the hazard lights.
answerASource: At a distance of around 100 m
answerBSource: At a distance of around 50 m
answerCSource: At a distance of around 10 m

questionTextSource: At night, how must you secure a vehicle which has broken down at a place where it constitutes a danger to other traffic?
explanationSource: If a vehicle has a breakdown on a multi-lane roadway at a location where it cannot be detected in time as an obstacle, you as a car driver must immediately turn on the hazard lights. Afterwards, a breakdown triangle should be placed clearly visible at a sufficient distance, in fast traffic about 100 m in front of the vehicle. You do not need to switch on the reversing light.
answerASource: Immediately switch on the hazard warning lights
answerBSource: Place a warning triangle at a sufficient distance
answerCSource: Switch on the reversing lights

questionTextSource: When must you switch on the hazard warning lights?
explanationSource: If a car breaks down on a multi-lane roadway at a location where it cannot be recognized in time as an obstacle, the hazard light must be switched on immediately. Parking in the second row is generally prohibited. This does not change just by switching on the hazard lights. It is not allowed. When loading and unloading the hazard light must not be switched on.
answerASource: When your vehicle has broken down at a place where it cannot readily be recognized as a stationary obstruction
answerBSource: When double-parking
answerCSource: For all loading or unloading

questionTextSource: What must you remember when towing?
explanationSource: When towing, the distance between the towing vehicle and the towed vehicle shall not exceed 5 meters. The tools used such as rod or rope are clearly marked. Motorcycles must not be towed but must be loaded.
answerASource: The distance between the vehicles must not exceed 5 m
answerBSource: Tow-rope/tow-bar must be clearly marked
answerCSource: Motorcycles may only be towed with a fixed connection

questionTextSource: Your car suffers a flat tyre on the motorway. What should you do?
explanationSource: If your vehicle breaks down on a multi-lane road due to a flat tire in a place where it cannot be detected in time as a stationary obstacle, immediately turn on the hazard lights. After that, a breakdown triangle is to be placed in a clearly visible position at a sufficient distance. In fast traffic, the distance between the vehicle and the breakdown triangle should be approx. 100 m.
answerASource: - turn on my hazard lights and park as far to the right as possible
answerBSource: - place the warning triangle at a distance of about 100 m
answerCSource: - continue driving to the next exit

questionTextSource: You car has a fault and can no longer be driven. What must you be aware of during towing?
explanationSource: Keep in mind that without the engine working, the brake boosters of the engine brake will also fail. This means that the applied braking force is much higher than when the engine is running. If possible, always use a tow bar when towing. The towing bar prevents collision and braking forces can be transmitted.
answerASource: - the force required to operate the brake pedal is significantly greater if the engine has failed
answerBSource: - a towing bar should be used where possible
answerCSource: - the rear fog lights should also be switched on to indicate the towing procedure

questionTextSource: You car has a fault and can no longer be driven. What must you be aware of during towing?
explanationSource: When towing, the towing vehicle and the towed vehicle must turn on the hazard lights. The tow rope should be kept as tight as possible. If the breakdown did not occur on the freeway, it is not allowed to drive onto the freeway during towing.
answerASource: - the hazard lights must be switched on on both vehicles
answerBSource: - the towing cable should be kept as taut as possible
answerCSource: - the motorway may only be used to shorten the journey to the next car garage

questionTextSource: You experience a breakdown outside of a built-up area. Your motor vehicle is located on the carriageway behind a bend in the road. What is the right course of action in this situation?
explanationSource: To secure the area, the car driver must turn on the hazard lights and set up a sign behind the vehicle to warn other road users. In addition, you should wear a safety vest for improved visibility.
answerASource: I have to turn on my hazard lights
answerBSource: I must at least position a conspicuous warning sign behind the vehicle
answerCSource: I should wear a high-visibility vest

questionTextSource: Your car has an engine fault and must be towed away with a tow rope. What must you be aware of?
