Before buying a bike rack for your pedelec for the trailer coupling, a few questions should be clarified. For example, is the support load of my trailer coupling sufficient at all and is the selected rear -bearing system designed for so much weight?
If both questions can be answered with yes and you have chosen a corresponding rear -bearing system, you have to adapt to the tire pressure about the additional loading.
answerASource: - the rear-mounted carrier system must be suitably designed
answerBSource: - the trailer coupling must have a suitable vertical load capacity
answerCSource: - the car’s tyre inflation pressure must be adjusted appropriately

questionTextSource: Which warning plate is correctly mounted?
explanationSource: As we need an A for (attention) the right warning sign is in place.
answerASource: The right one
answerBSource: The left one

questionTextSource: How can you determine that a truck is overloaded?
explanationSource: To determine an overload, you can weigh again your loaded vehicle or by built-axle load measuring devices.
answerASource: By weighing the loaded vehicle
answerBSource: From built-in axle weight measuring devices
answerCSource: By doing a brake test with the loaded vehicle

questionTextSource: A truck for exchangeable load carriers (interchangeable containers) has a permissable total mass of 6,000 kg. The interchangeable container has an empty mass of 1,500 kg. Which mass are you allowed to stow in this interchangeable container?
explanationSource: Empty weight (1500 kg) plus loading (4,500 kg) = payload (6000 kg) 4,500 kg is correct. In other words, payload (6000 kg) minus the empty weight (1500 kg) of the interchangeable container gives the load (4500 kg)

questionTextSource: What do you understand by form-fit secured load?
explanationSource: Under a form-locking securing loads (zero) means the application of charge to loading limitations and secured spacers and the direct support cargo against frontal and side walls. The package has nothing to do.
answerASource: Positioning the load directly against front and side walls
answerBSource: The load touches the load area limits and secured spacer blocks
answerCSource: Packaging that is adapted to the shape of the loaded goods

questionTextSource: What do you understand by form-fit securing of the load?
explanationSource: The positive load securing means that free spaces are closed between the cargo items by means of suitable filler, and the load is located directly on the end and side wall.
answerASource: The load is positioned directly at the front and side wall
answerBSource: Free space between parts of the load must be filled with appropriate filling
answerCSource: The free-standing load is lashed down to the loading area by appropriate lashing means

questionTextSource: Who is responsible for the load being sufficiently secured?
explanationSource: All the transport stakeholders are responsible for adequate cargo securing.
answerASource: All parties involved in the transport process
answerBSource: Only the driver
answerCSource: Only the loader

questionTextSource: Who is responsible for operating vehicles in regard to its load?
explanationSource: both the driver and the holder will be responsible for the operation of the vehicles.
answerASource: The driver
answerBSource: The owner
answerCSource: The registration authority

questionTextSource: What do you have to consider when tipping bulk goods?
explanationSource: When dumping of materials, please make sure that the vehicle is on sufficiently solid ground, and protective must be released so that the dumping body can be fully tilted.
answerASource: The vehicle must stand on sufficiently firm ground
answerBSource: The relevant safety devices must be unlocked to allow the tipping bridge to be completely tilted
answerCSource: The side walls should only be opened when the tipping bridge is in its tilted end position

questionTextSource: Why is it necessary to observe the load distribution plan?
explanationSource: In order to avoid exceeding the axle load, it is necessary to adhere to the load distribution plan. So you can also ensure the steering ability.
answerASource: To prevent exceeding the axle loads
answerBSource: To ensure steering capacity is maintained
answerCSource: To make loading and unloading easier

questionTextSource: What is the purpose of a load distribution plan?
explanationSource: The load distribution plan shows how the vehicle must be loaded. If the distribution is uneven, the braking behaviour changes greatly.
answerASource: To help with the road-worthy loading of the vehicle
answerBSource: To avoid falling below the axle load limit of the vehicle
answerCSource: To determine the unloading sequence for the vehicle

questionTextSource: What could happen if your vehicle is not loaded according to the load distribution plan?
explanationSource: The load distribution plan shows how the vehicle must be loaded. If the load is unevenly distributed, the braking behaviour will change considerably. Damage to tyres occurs and steering ability is also impaired.
answerASource: The tyres may become damaged
answerBSource: The steering ability may be affected
answerCSource: Its braking performance may be adversely affected

questionTextSource: What specific information can you find in a load distribution plan?
explanationSource: The load distribution plan gives information on the load centre of gravity with the maximum permissible load.
answerASource: Information about the centre of gravity of the load at the maximum allowable load limit
answerBSource: Information about securing the load on the load platform
answerCSource: Information about the maximum height of the load on the load platform

questionTextSource: Who is responsible for securing the load on the vehicle?
answerASource: The driver
answerBSource: The shipper
answerCSource: The receiver

questionTextSource: What can help with friction-locked load securing?
explanationSource:
In force-locking load securing, the transported goods are pressed onto the loading surface by means of lashing. This type of lashing is also known as tie-down lashing.
The load usually stands freely on the loading surface and is only secured by the increased force applied. In most cases, the frictional force between the loading surface and the load is increased.
The load is secured with the aid of load securing equipment such as lashing straps, lashing chains, anti-slip mats or cover nets and with the aid of attachment points on the loading surface.
This prevents the load from slipping.
answerASource: Lashing straps
answerBSource: Anti-slip mats
answerCSource: Lashing chains

questionTextSource: What should be taken into consideration when selecting load-securing lashing equipment?
explanationSource:
Among other things, the face and shape of the load are decisive for the selection of suitable and necessary lashing equipment. 
Equally important are the requirements of the vehicle, such as the permissible payload and the attachment points.
answerASource: The shape of the load
answerBSource: The weight of the load
answerCSource: The available lashing points

questionTextSource: You want to secure your load by lashing it down. What should you bear in mind?
explanationSource: When lashing down, the load is pressed onto the loading area using lashing devices to increase the friction force and prevent slipping. It is important to check the tension of the lashing devices, as the necessary contact force and thus the required friction can only be achieved with sufficient tension. In addition, this method is only suitable for dimensionally stable loads; flexible goods can deform under the tension, which means that the pre-tensioning force is lost and load securing is no longer guaranteed.
answerASource: - regularly check the tension of the lashing equipment
answerBSource: - only secure dimensionally stable loads by lashing them down
answerCSource: - choose the smallest possible lashing angle

questionTextSource: What is better for reasons of driving safety?
explanationSource: Since you could load (truck) in a truck with a trailer on the front part of the weight, it is better for the sake of driving safety when the trailer is empty.
answerASource: The truck is loaded and the trailer is empty
answerBSource: The trailer is loaded and the truck is empty

questionTextSource: What must you remember when you wish to transport animals on a trailer?
explanationSource: You have to make sure that the maximum authorized mass of the trailer is not exceeded and subject to special rules for the transport of animals in an animal transportation.
answerASource: The special regulations for the transport of animals
answerBSource: The total permissible mass of the trailer must not be exceeded
answerCSource: Animals may only be transported on trailers with air-brakes

questionTextSource: When must attached working equipment be marked with red-white warning plates?
explanationSource: A red and white warning board is then to mount when it protrudes rearward more than 1 m on the rear lamps of the tractor if it has traffic hazardous parts and also when it laterally by more than 40 cm beyond the boundary or rear lamps of the tractor protrudes. All three are correct.
answerASource: When it has parts presenting a danger to traffic
answerBSource: When extending laterally more than 40 cm beyond the side lights or rear lights of the tractor
answerCSource: When extending more than 1 m to the back beyond the rear lights of the tractor

questionTextSource: What duties do you have when driving a loaded vehicle?
explanationSource: If you lead a loaded vehicle, you are responsible for ensuring that the safety of the vehicle is not affected by the charge and you have to adjust your speed to the load conditions.

The headlights should also be checked for defects and their correct setting. For example, headlights that are set too high can dazzle oncoming traffic.

questionTextSource: What do you do within this danger section?
explanationSource: Attention wild animals crossing: This sign is set up on routes, where a lot of wild animal accidents happened already. As soon as you see this sign, you should reduce the speed and be ready to brake. Pay particular attention to the roadside. If you see an animal, you should brake and dim. If game suddenly jumps in front of you, you should make an emergency brake. Avoid dodging, because then you might get into the oncoming lane.
answerASource: Drive more slowly, watch out for wild animals
answerBSource: Dip the headlights and brake when you see wild animals
answerCSource: Do not avoid hitting wild animals if oncoming traffic would be endangered

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for upon seeing this traffic sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates a section of the route with crosswinds. This is especially true for bridges and overpasses. If there's strong wind from the side you have to reduce your speed. You need to be prepared to hold on and countersteer to stay in the lane. The traffic sign has nothing to do with air traffic.
answerASource: For side winds
answerBSource: For counter-steering
answerCSource: For aircraft overhead

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for upon seeing this traffic sign?
explanationSource: If you encounter this traffic sign, you have to adjust to strong crosswinds. Most commonly, this traffic sign appears on road sections that are particularly exposed to the wind - for example, on bridges or overpasses. With strong crosswinds, the driver must countersteer in order to stay in the lane. You also need to adjust the speed of your car to the wind conditions.
answerASource: For an alteration in the responsiveness of the vehicle
answerBSource: For the necessity for counter-steering
answerCSource: For the necessity for adjusting speed

questionTextSource: What should you anticipate when you see this road sign?
explanationSource: Because of trees, tall vehicles can brake suddenly and swerve. Tall vehicles can tear off branches as they pass, and these then lie on the roadway. Be especially attentive.
answerASource: Vehicles ahead that may swerve out suddenly to the left
answerBSource: Vehicles ahead that may brake suddenly
answerCSource: Tree branches lying on the roadway

questionTextSource: What must you anticipate with this road sign?
explanationSource: All 3 answers are correct. Trees may lie in the driveway, branches may lie in the road, and cars may suddenly give way.
answerASource: Trees could protrude into my road area
answerBSource: There could be branches lying on the roadway
answerCSource: Vehicles could swerve suddenly

questionTextSource: What is correct from this traffic sign onwards?
explanationSource: This sign indicates that no vehicles can proceed, you therefore cannot ride your motorized bicycle but can still push it
answerASource: You may push your motorized bicycle
answerBSource: You may drive on slowly with your motorized bicycle
answerCSource: You may proceed with your motorized bicycle when the engine is switched off

explanationSource: The total mass (12 t) refers to the vehicle including charge.

answerBSource: - of the vehicle excluding load
answerCSource: - of the load excluding the vehicle

explanationSource: The length of your truck is described as 10.6 m. (10 m + 60 cm truck of the load) which dominates. That's why you must not enter this road as a whole, the length of your truck 10.6 m.

answerCSource: - drive into a street displaying this sign, because the load does not have to be taken into consideration

questionTextSource: You are driving on this motorway. What is the right course of action?
explanationSource:
In principle, the right-hand driving rule applies on motorways. You are therefore obliged to drive in the right-hand lane unless you want to overtake. There is no one in front of you to overtake.
The left lane is closed. You may not use it under any circumstances. Not even if you have a breakdown.
answerASource: I move into the right-hand lane
answerBSource: I only use the left-hand lane if there is a breakdown
answerCSource: I remain in my current lane

questionTextSource: What is the right behaviour now?
explanationSource: You are allowed to accelerate because you have passed the construction site with the speed limit, moved back into the correct lane and the road in front of you is clear.
answerASource: I accelerate
answerBSource: I maintain my speed
answerCSource: I only accelerate when the speed limit is removed by a road sign

questionTextSource: What must you remember when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates that the following route can only be used with snow chains. Driving without snow chains is prohibited. The maximum speed with snow chains is 50 km/h. Pay attention to noises. If you hear something or if your car is twitchy you should stop and check the chains. This sign has nothing to do with track vehicles.
answerASource: Use snow-chains
answerBSource: Maximum permissible speed 50 km/h
answerCSource: Track-vehicles have priority

questionTextSource: Which vehicles are prohibited from using a road sign-posted like this?
explanationSource: The traffic sign prohibits vehicles from traveling on the route that exceed the weight indicated on the traffic sign. The weight specification refers to the actual axle load. It must not be exceeded. The red round circle in the traffic sign always indicates a ban.
answerASource: - with an actual axle weight exceeding 2 t
answerBSource: - with a permissible axle weight exceeding 2 t
answerCSource: - with a permissible total weight not exceeding 2 t

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign "Width Limit including load" indicates road constrictions and bottlenecks on the subsequent section of the road. The track is not passable for vehicles that are wider than indicated on the sign including their cargo. In this case, the permissible width is 2 m.
answerASource: A prohibition for vehicles more than 2 m wide including load
answerBSource: A roadway 2 m wide
answerCSource: You must keep a lateral distance of at least 2 m from other vehicles

questionTextSource: Which vehicles can cause serious accidents when this traffic sign is ignored?
explanationSource: This traffic sign is aimed at trucks and warns of height-limited passages - in front of tunnels, bridge constructions or heavy current overhead wires of trams. The height limit is indicated by the black triangles, here it is 3.8 m. If a vehicle higher than 3.8 m ignores this sign, serious accidents may occur. The truck may get caught or stuck in the overhead wires or the underpass. Length and width are not relevant.
answerASource: Length 8 m, width 2.5 m, height 4 m
answerBSource: Length 10 m, width 3.9 m, height 3 m
answerCSource: Length 4 m, width 2.5 m, height 2.5 m

questionTextSource: With which vehicles are you not allowed to drive on a road sign-posted like this?
explanationSource: The red round circle in the traffic sign always indicates a ban. Vehicles whose actual length exceeds 10 m may not drive on this road. Even vehicles with trailers whose total length exceeds 10 m may not continue on this road. This indicates road conditions that are not passable with longer vehicles.
answerASource: Car with caravan trailer, total length of the combination 11 m
answerBSource: Tractor with trailer, total length of the combination 12 m
answerCSource: Truck with a length of 9 m including load

questionTextSource: What vehicles may you overtake when this traffic sign is displayed?
explanationSource: You are not allowed to overtake, neither single nor multi-lane vehicles.
answerASource: No vehicles

answerCSource: Multi-track vehicles

answerBSource: Single-track motor vehicles
answerCSource: Cars without trailers

answerBSource: Truck tractors
answerCSource: Cars

questionTextSource: Which vehicles are not allowed to overtake here?
explanationSource: Trucks and trucks with a gross vehicle mass exceeding 3.5 tonnes and tractors may not overtake at the sign.
answerASource: Trucks and trucks with trailers with a permissible total mass exceeding 3.5 t

answerCSource: Cars

questionTextSource: You are driving a vehicle combination comprising a motor vehicle and trailer, and you want to enter a road that has this signpost. What does the reference to “5.5 t” mean?
explanationSource: The traffic sign refers to the actual mass of each individual vehicle, the vehicle combination, including charge.

answerBSource: - actual total mass of the vehicle combination
answerCSource: - maximum authorised mass of the towing vehicle

questionTextSource: When you see this road sign, what is the situation in which you must keep a minimum distance of 70 metres from the vehicle in front?
explanationSource: As a driver of a truck with a maximum permissible mass of over 3.5 tonnes or as a driver of a tractor, I must keep a distance of at least 70 meters if there is a truck in front of me with a permissible maximum mass of over 3.5 tonnes.
answerASource: - am driving a lorry with a permissible total weight of over 3.5 t behind another lorry with a permissible total weight of over 3.5 t
answerBSource: - am driving a tractor unit behind a lorry with a permissible total weight of over 3.5 t
answerCSource: - am driving a lorry with a permissible total weight of over 3.5 t behind a car with a trailer

questionTextSource: What motor vehicles are prohibited from driving on a road where this sign is displayed?
explanationSource:
This road sign describes a driving ban for tractors with a design-related maximum speed up to 25 km / h, as well as for motor vehicles with a trailer with a permissible maximum speed up to 25 km / h. 
This does not apply to tractors with a maximum design speed of more than 40 km/h.
answerASource: Tractors with a type-specific maximum speed of 25 km/h
answerBSource: Motor vehicles with trailers with a maximum permissible speed of 25 km/h
answerCSource: Tractors with a type-specific maximum speed in excess of 40 km/h

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign is at the end of the motorway.
answerASource: The end of the autobahn
answerBSource: The end of the clearway for motor vehicles only
answerCSource: Temporary closure of the autobahn

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign is placed at the end of a highway. It means: From here on the advisory speed limit no longer applies. It does not indicate a ban on motor vehicles.
answerASource: The end of the clearway for motor vehicles only
answerBSource: The end of the autobahn
answerCSource: Motor vehicles prohibited

questionTextSource: What do such traffic signs indicate?
explanationSource: A white number on a blue background with a white outline indicates the number of the motorway you are currently on.
answerASource: The number of an autobahn
answerBSource: The distance to the next petrol station
answerCSource: The number of a European road

questionTextSource: What does the figure "26" indicate in this traffic sign?
explanationSource: All motorway exits are numbered for better orientation. As a car driver, you can recognize the freeway exit ramp by the number 26. On road maps, in road atlases and in the common navigation devices, these numbers can also be found again.
answerASource: Autobahn exit number 26
answerBSource: The A 26
answerCSource: The distance from the exit to Düsseldorf-Benrath

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign refers to a detour for freeway traffic. It is set up in the event of a traffic jam, for example. If the car driver follows the detour, he/she returns to the freeway.
answerASource: A diversion for autobahn traffic when required
answerBSource: An underground station
answerCSource: Federal road (Bundesstrasse) 22

questionTextSource: What applies from this traffic sign onwards?
explanationSource: This traffic sign is subject to regulations that apply to traffic within built-up areas. This includes limiting the maximum speed to 50 km/h. Free choice of lanes applies only to vehicles up to 3.5 t permissible maximum mass. Vehicles with more than 3.5 t drive on the right.
answerASource: Maximum permissible speed 50 km/h
answerBSource: Free choice of marked driving lanes for motor vehicles with a permissible total mass up to 3.5 t
answerCSource: Free choice of marked driving lanes for all motor vehicles

questionTextSource: What applies when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: In towns, you are allowed to deviate from the requirement to drive on the right, i.e. you are free to choose your lane on multi-lane roads. On two-lane roads in towns, vehicles in the right lane may also drive faster than vehicles in the left lane, but you are still not allowed to drive faster than 50 km/h in towns.
answerASource: - choose whatever lane I wish for my direction of travel
answerBSource: - drive more quickly in the right-hand lane than vehicles travelling in the left-hand lane
answerCSource: - drive faster than 50 km/h because this is a multi-lane carriageway

questionTextSource: What applies from this traffic sign onwards?
explanationSource: Car drivers recognize from this traffic sign that the regulations for traffic within closed villages apply. You must not announce overtaking maneuvers by honking, as this is only allowed outside closed villages. In general, it is forbidden to drive with parking lights. Parking cars may be secured with the parking light.
answerASource: You may not sound your horn to signal your intention to overtake
answerBSource: Parking cars may be safely marked with parking light
answerCSource: You may drive with parking light in the darkness

questionTextSource: How are vehicles with a permissible total mass up to 2.8 t allowed to be parked here?
explanationSource: This traffic sign signals to the vehicle driver that vehicles up to 2.8 t of permissible total mass may only be parked in accordance with the illustration. However, smaller cars also have to comply with these regulations as well.
answerASource: The car as shown in the picture
answerBSource: The caravan as shown in the picture
answerCSource: Small cars also diagonally to the driving direction

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The square traffic sign with a blue border indicates that there is a refuel possibility for hydrogen-powered vehicles in the immediate vicinity. These cars are still relatively expensive, but are considered the vehicles of the future.
answerASource: - hydrogen filling station
answerBSource: - natural gas fuel station
answerCSource: - auto gas filling station

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign points out a natural gas filling station where drivers can refuel their natural gas-powered vehicle. The abbreviation CNG stands for the term compressed natural gas.
answerASource: - natural gas fuel station
answerBSource: - hydrogen filling station
answerCSource: - auto gas filling station

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The inscription LPG on the square traffic sign is an abbreviation for Liquefied Petroleum Gas. This traffic sign signals to the car driver that there is an LPG station.
answerASource: - auto gas filling station
answerBSource: - natural gas fuel station
answerCSource: - hydrogen filling station

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: You will see a plug with cable on the sign. It shows you a nearby charging station for electric vehicles such as electric cars, e-bikes or electric scooters. There you can charge your powered vehicle.
answerASource: A charging station for electric vehicles
answerBSource: A danger of explosion at a filling station through electric devices
answerCSource: A facility for charging mobile telephones

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign P + R on a blue background stands for "Park and Ride". It identifies a parking lot with public transport connection. The parking lot is continuous or temporary.
answerASource: A carpark with connections to public transport ("Park + Ride")
answerBSource: A filling station with carwash ("Care + Clean")
answerCSource: A car hire centre with repair workshop ("Breakdown service + Repair")

questionTextSource: You are driving a toll-chargeable truck and are approaching the displayed traffic sign. What should you do?
explanationSource: You can only continue if the toll was paid for using the route and you should check whether it is correctly set the correct number of axles here without on-board unit (OBU). If not, ask me correctly.
answerASource: I check the on-board-unit (OBU), to see if the number of axles is set correctly
answerBSource: I may continue driving without an on-board-unit (OBU), only if a toll was paid for using the route
answerCSource: I pay the toll at the toll station upon leaving the toll route

questionTextSource: What is correct in this situation?
explanationSource: Driving a protective strip continuously is not permitted. Protective strips have a dashed mark and can be driven by car if necessary, especially to avoid oncoming traffic. But you can only do that if bicycle traffic is not endangered.
answerASource: - allowed to drive on the advisory bike lane after I have ruled out any risk to cyclists by carefully observing the road traffic situation
answerBSource: - allowed to drive on the advisory bike lane only until I am past the truck
answerCSource: - not allowed to drive on the advisory bike lane

questionTextSource: Which document allows you to park here?
explanationSource: You are allowed to park in the parking lot with this sign if you yourself have an EU parking permit for severely disabled people or if you are transporting a person with an EU parking permit for severely disabled people.
answerASource: My EU parking card for people with severe disabilities
answerBSource: The EU parking card of a severely disabled person whom I am transporting
answerCSource: My residential parking permit

questionTextSource: You need assistance on the autobahn. Which information does the arrow on the roadside marker give you?
explanationSource: Arrows on the reflector posts on the edge of a freeway point towards the nearest emergency services pillar. If you need help, follow the arrows to reach an emergency pillar as quickly as possible.
answerASource: Nearest emergency call telephone in the direction of the arrow
answerBSource: Nearest car park in the direction of the arrow
answerCSource: Next exit in the direction of the arrow

questionTextSource: What can you do to avoid unnecessary noise?
explanationSource: To avoid unnececessary noise, avoid making high revolutions with your engine and do not modify either the intake/exhaust system. Removing fairings do not impact noise.
answerASource: Avoid high engine revolutions
answerBSource: Do not modify intake and exhaust systems
answerCSource: Remove the fairings

questionTextSource: What can you do to avoid noise pollution?
explanationSource: To avoid above normal level of noise called noise pollution, do all of the above.
answerASource: Don't drive off with spinning wheels
answerBSource: Avoid "power sound" through loudspeakers
answerCSource: Push the vehicle on residential premises, if possible

questionTextSource: How can you save fuel?
explanationSource: You as a car driver can save fuel when you are traveling with your vehicle at low speeds. You should shift as early as possible into a higher gear and switch back as late as possible to keep the engine speeds low.
answerASource: Change to a higher gear earlier
answerBSource: Change to a lower gear later
answerCSource: By driving frequently at high revolutions

questionTextSource: How can noise be avoided?
explanationSource: Noise is generally generated by the running engine of a car. Driving at high speeds and strong acceleration contributes to increased noise. Stop the engine if you wait or park your vehicle somewhere for a longer amount of time.
answerASource: If possible, avoid strong acceleration
answerBSource: By switching off the engine if you have to wait or stop for a longer period
answerCSource: By driving at high revolutions

questionTextSource: How is avoidable noise caused?
explanationSource: Avoidable noise can be caused by driving with a defective exhaust system or by "playing" with the gas while standing. If a car driver honks because of a dangerous situation, this does not count as avoidable noise, but serves to avert danger.
answerASource: By driving with a defective exhaust
answerBSource: By "playing" with the accelerator when standing still
answerCSource: By sounding your horn when in danger

questionTextSource: Why should you avoid driving off at a rapid pace?
explanationSource: Making a racing start is an additional noise pollution for other road users. You as a car driver damage your vehicle as well as the tires due to high wear and abrasion with a racing start. The environment is additionally burdened. On the other hand, the rear-wheel brake is no longer and not any less loaded when starting.
answerASource: Because the noise you cause annoys others
answerBSource: Because of heavier tyre wear
answerCSource: Because this is a great strain on the rear brake

questionTextSource: How can you avoid pollution of the environment and benefit your engine after a cold start?
explanationSource: If possible, do not allow your vehicle to warm up while you are stationary as this will damage the engine and the environment. If warming up is done at low engine speeds, you will help to protect both. The engine is only fully resilient after reaching the operating temperature (green area).
answerASource: Set off at low revolutions per minute without running the engine until it warms up
answerBSource: Do not accelerate, let the engine warm up while the vehicle is stationary
answerCSource: Accelerate several times when stationary so that a favourable operation temperature is quickly reached

questionTextSource: Which vehicles are allowed to enter this environmental zone?
explanationSource: Motor vehicle drivers are only allowed to drive their vehicles into an environmental zone if the vehicle has a valid sticker visibly attached to it, which at least corresponds to the qualifying plate (red, yellow, green). Motorcycles and vehicles with special rights are excluded from this regulation.
answerASource: A car with a red emissions sticker
answerBSource: A motorcycle
answerCSource: A car which meets the requirements for a green sticker even if it does not have an emissions sticker

questionTextSource: What is caused by driving with winter tyres during the summer months?
explanationSource: Every car driver should know that winter tires are designed for use in cold and snowy streets. By using them in summer months, the driving stability decreases. Greater rubber abrasion and tire wear lead to increased fuel consumption.
answerASource: - the fuel consumption
answerBSource: - tyre wear
answerCSource: - driving stability

questionTextSource: What motor vehicles may enter an environment zone that has this sign?
explanationSource: The additional sign shows which motor vehicles with which sticker may enter. Motorbikes may enter in any case.
answerASource: Motor vehicles with a green particulate emissions sticker
answerBSource: Motorcycles
answerCSource: Motor vehicles that meet the requirements for a green particulate emissions sticker, even if no such sticker is attached

questionTextSource: You want to wash your car. Where should you do this in the interests of environmental protection?
explanationSource: You should always wash your vehicle in a car wash. There, the wastewater is disposed of by the operators in an environmentally friendly manner. On the other hand, if you wash your vehicle on the road or in a parking lot, the water gets into the ground untreated.
answerASource: At a carwash
answerBSource: In a carpark
answerCSource: On an open road

questionTextSource: What increases the fuel consumption of your vehicle considerably?
explanationSource: Turning on the seat heating or air conditioning of your vehicle consumes a lot of energy and increases the fuel consumption of your car significantly. Navigation devices require relatively little power compared to these devices.
answerASource: - heated seats
answerBSource: - the air-conditioning
answerCSource: - the navigation device

questionTextSource: What can reduce the fuel consumption of a commercial vehicle?
explanationSource: A reduction can cause all three answers. The use of louvers on the vehicle, the driver & predictive operation, even the use of special tires.
answerASource: Fitting air cowls to the vehicle
answerBSource: The farsighted driving style of the driver
answerCSource: The use of special tyres

questionTextSource: What are the effects of driving at high speed in motor vehicles with combustion engines?
explanationSource: Be aware that fast, high-speed driving results in high fuel consumption and high exhaust emissions. This also leads to more noise and to a not lower noise level.
answerASource: Fuel consumption increases
answerBSource: The emission of harmful substances increases
answerCSource: The noise level is reduced

questionTextSource: When riding a motorcycle, what can you do to avoid unnecessary noise?
explanationSource:
Improper exhaust systems lead to higher noise pollution. 
Uneven driving, such as frequent braking and then accelerating hard again, leads to unnecessary noise.
High-speed starting leads to unnecessary noise.
answerASource: By only using permissible exhaust systems
answerBSource: By driving off at high speed
answerCSource: By adopting an uneven driving style

questionTextSource: Why is an environmentally considerate driving style also beneficial for road safety?
explanationSource: Alternative fuels can also be used to drive fast. However, you will be driving in an environmentally friendly way if you avoid high speeds, if you drive with foresight and thus avoid, for example, braking hard and starting again.
answerASource: - driving in a far-sighted manner
answerBSource: - avoiding high speeds
answerCSource: - that with alternative fuels it is only possible to drive slowly

questionTextSource: What must you know about catalytic converters?
explanationSource: Vehicles with a catalytic converter should not be towed away. As a result, unburned fuel can flow into the catalytic converter and lead to damage or destruction of the catalyst. If the engine of the vehicle does not start even after several start attempts, the same consequences can occur.
answerASource: A catalytic converter can be damaged or destroyed when the car is towed to start
answerBSource: A catalytic converter can be damaged when the engine starts after many unsuccessful attempts
answerCSource: Unburned fuel in the catalytic converter cannot cause any damage when the engine starts

questionTextSource: Tyre wear is a burden to the environment. What can extend tyre life?
explanationSource: The wear of tires is the lowest when the tires are used as intended. Winter tires that are used in summer wear faster due to their material properties (rubber compound). Even with too little air pressure tires wear faster. Starting with high-speed and squealing tires wears tires due to increased abrasion.
answerASource: Avoiding high speeds
answerBSource: Observing the tyre pressure indicated in the owner's manual
answerCSource: Using winter tyres (M+S) in the summer

questionTextSource: How can you save fuel when driving a motor vehicle with automatic transmission?
explanationSource: Avoid the kick-down. The high-speed acceleration gear causes increased fuel consumption. For overtaking or merging on the freeway, however, it can be useful. With P, Parking, a vehicle with automatic transmission cannot be driven. Changing gears early to a higher gear also saves fuel.
answerASource: By not using the "kick down" facility, if possible
answerBSource: When driving in town traffic, always keep the selector lever in the lowest gear
answerCSource: By driving in gear "P", if possible

questionTextSource: How can you keep the burden on the environment low?
explanationSource: In order to relieve the environment, you should form carpools with others as a car driver. The environment is particularly burdened by the number of moving vehicles. In addition, you should avoid a concentrated accumulation of several vehicles such as traffic jams.
answerASource: By forming car pools
answerBSource: By avoiding peak traffic periods
answerCSource: By avoiding roads with frequent traffic jams

questionTextSource: What results in increased fuel consumption of your car?
explanationSource: Increased fuel consumption can be expected if, for example, you install roof or ski racks or if you load your vehicle with unnecessary objects. Increased tire pressure, on the other hand, does not matter for fuel consumption, even if it should be avoided altogether.
answerASource: A roof rack or ski rack
answerBSource: Transporting unnecessary objects
answerCSource: Excessive tyre pressure

questionTextSource: How can you save fuel?
explanationSource: Additional load and additional attachments such as roof racks or ski holders increase fuel consumption: when not in use, remove them. You save fuel when driving low-speed: shift up the gear early. Turning off the air conditioning system saves fuel, as does maintaining tire pressure according to the manufacturer's specifications. Because too little tire pressure increases the rolling resistance.
answerASource: By observing at least the tyre pressure indicated in the owner's manual
answerBSource: By removing roof racks or ski racks
answerCSource: Avoid driving in top gear

questionTextSource: What should you avoid for reasons of environmental protection?
explanationSource: After starting the engine, you as a car driver should start immediately in a low-speed driving style. Warming up the engine while stationary is not permitted. Driving at high speeds, extending gears and switching late, wastes fuel. Unnecessary driving causes avoidable exhaust emissions.
answerASource: Letting the engine warm up when the vehicle is stationary
answerBSource: Unnecessary trips
answerCSource: Loud music with windows opened

questionTextSource: Which conduct shows awareness of the environment?
explanationSource: Avoid driving around aimlessly. Private transport pollutes the environment. Make carpools as often as you can. If possible, use public transport. If you are stuck in a traffic jam, do not run the engine unnecessarily, turn it off.
answerASource: Changing to public transport at collective parking areas ("P + R")
answerBSource: The formation of car pools
answerCSource: Switching off the engine in a traffic jam

questionTextSource: How can you reduce noise?
explanationSource: Driving in the high-speed range causes more noise than a low-speed driving style. In built-up areas you should drive in the highest possible gear, so that the engine causes the least possible noise. Avoid a high-speed start with squeaky tires.
answerASource: By driving in 4th or 5th gear in built-up areas
answerBSource: By driving at high engine revolutions
answerCSource: By driving at high speed

questionTextSource: What does "power sound" in the car result in?
explanationSource: "Power sound" in the car causes over-stimulation. This over-stimulation may distract the car driver so that he/she may miss important other signals. Bear in mind that other road users are also bothered by the noise.
answerASource: Signals from other traffic - in particular sirens - are not heard
answerBSource: There is noise pollution for others even when the windows are closed
answerCSource: Driving errors due to the driver being diverted

questionTextSource: By which percentage does the fuel consumption (l/100 km) of a middle-class car driving at 160 km/h in general increase compared to driving at the recommended speed of 130 km/h?
explanationSource: Compared to driving at the advisory speed limit of 130 km/h, the fuel consumption at a speed of 160 km/h usually increases very much. For example, the consumption of a mid-range car can rise by up to 35%.
answerASource: By up to 35 %
answerBSource: By up to 5 %
answerCSource: By up to 10 %

questionTextSource: When are you allowed to enter an environmental zone?
explanationSource: You may only drive into an environmental zone as a car driver if a valid badge is visibly attached to your vehicle that at least corresponds to the qualifying plate (red, yellow, green). Excluded from this rule are exclusively vehicles with special rights, for example historic motor vehicles.
answerASource: When your vehicle has a corresponding emissions sticker
answerBSource: When your vehicle enjoys special rights
answerCSource: When you have a low-emission vehicle

questionTextSource: Where do you have to place the emissions sticker for identifying lowemission vehicles?
explanationSource: As a car driver, you must ensure that the carbon emission sticker is affixed to the inside of the windshield of your vehicle so that it clearly identifies your low-emission vehicle.
answerASource: Clearly visible on the inside of the windscreen
answerBSource: Clearly visible on the outside of the windscreen
answerCSource: Clearly visible on the inside of the rear window

questionTextSource: If your car has a petrol engine but does not have an emissions sticker, are you allowed to enter an environmental zone which is sign-posted as follows?
explanationSource: Without visibly attached carbon emission sticker, motor vehicle drivers are not allowed to drive into an environmental zone with a gasoline engine. The badge on the vehicle must be valid and correspond at least to the qualifying plate (red, yellow, green).
answerASource: No, because my car does not have an emissions sticker
answerBSource: Yes, because I am driving a car with catalytic converter with lambda control
answerCSource: Yes, because this prohibition only applies to diesel vehicles

questionTextSource: Where can you stow your baggage so that you do not increase your fuel consumption unnecessarily?
explanationSource: If possible, you should only store the luggage in the trunk of your vehicle. The use of a roof rack to carry luggage results in greatly increased aerodynamic drag, often resulting in a significant increase in fuel consumption.
answerASource: In the boot
answerBSource: Behind the front seats
answerCSource: On the roof-rack

questionTextSource: Tyre wear negatively impacts the environment. How can you reduce this impact?
explanationSource: To reduce tyre wear (i.e. the wear on the tyres), you can drive with foresight and significantly minimise the load by regularly checking the tyre pressure in accordance with the manufacturer's specifications. Less wear = less load.
answerASource: By employing an anticipatory driving style
answerBSource: By regularly checking the tyre inflation pressure in accordance with the manufacturer's guidelines
answerCSource: By using winter tyres in summer

questionTextSource: What can contribute to environmentally-friendly driving?
explanationSource: If possible, carpool to reduce the number of vehicles on the road. Training can help you to adopt a fuel-efficient driving style. Electric vehicles do not emit any pollutants, so they are particularly environmentally friendly when driving.
answerASource: Attending a practical driving training session for a fuel-efficient driving style
answerBSource: Using vehicles with an electric drive
answerCSource: Forming car pools

questionTextSource: How can you save fuel if you drive a car with an automatic gearbox?
explanationSource: Unnecessary acceleration leads to high fuel consumption. Drive in eco mode if available. The car adapts its acceleration behaviour to reduce fuel consumption.
answerASource: Do not press down hard on the accelerator when moving off
answerBSource: Use the eco mode when driving
answerCSource: Switch off the engine when driving down a gradient

questionTextSource: What cars that are driven within designated environmental zones must display a particulate matter sticker?
explanationSource: In principle, all cars in a designated environmental zone are subject to the obligation to display a fine dust sticker. Work machines, ambulances, etc. are exempt.
answerASource: - petrol engines
answerBSource: - diesel engines
answerCSource: - electric motors

questionTextSource: What can result from listening to very loud music while driving a car?
explanationSource:
Too loud music can cause warning signals and the like of other road users to be overheard. Too loud music can also annoy, disturb and thus endanger other road users.
The driver's ability to react deteriorates due to the loud music and the resulting reduced ability to concentrate.
answerASource: The driver fails to detect signals from other traffic
answerBSource: It causes a nuisance to other road users
answerCSource: The driver’s responsiveness is enhanced

questionTextSource: Air resistance affects the fuel consumption of a vehicle. What affects air resistance the most?
explanationSource:
The faster you drive, the greater the air resistance acting on your vehicle. Doubling your speed results in 4 times the air resistance. 
The tread depth of the tyres has a major effect on the rolling resistance of the tyres.
The number of passengers does not directly affect the air resistance.
answerASource: The speed travelled
answerBSource: The number of passengers
answerCSource: The profile depth of the tyres

questionTextSource: You have to wait because a rail-crossing barrier is lowered. How can you now help protect the environment at this point?
explanationSource: By using the automatic start-stop system, the vehicle can be switched off while waiting and immediately switched on again with little effort. This minimises the emission of pollutants. Applying the handbrake has no effect on the vehicle's pollutant emissions.
answerASource: By using the automatic start-stop system
answerBSource: By putting the vehicle in first gear
answerCSource: By activating the parking brake

questionTextSource: What increases the range of an electric vehicle?
explanationSource: If you only brake slightly, the electric motor brakes and the kinetic energy is recovered. If you brake harder, you will be mechanically braked and the kinetic energy will not be recovered. Due to a high recuperation level, an electric car behaves like a combustion engine, so you may have to brake less strongly, i.e. mechanically. The heating in the electric car is fed directly from the battery and thus consumes energy that could also be used for driving.
answerASource: Using a high energy recovery (recuperation) level
answerBSource: Reducing the heater temperature
answerCSource: Short, sharp acceleration

questionTextSource: What is the benefit of recuperation in an electric vehicle?
explanationSource: Recuperation means "recovery". Just as electrical energy was converted into kinetic energy, braking converts the kinetic energy back into electrical energy, which is then fed back into the battery. This increases the range of the electric vehicle.
answerASource: - increases range
answerBSource: - conserves the service brake system
answerCSource: - means that energy can be recovered from the braking operation

questionTextSource: Your car is equipped with an automatic start-stop system. What is the environmental protection benefit from using this vehicle system?
explanationSource:
The automatic start-stop system automatically switches off the engine when you come to a standstill and restarts it as soon as you press one of the pedals. 
By switching off the engine, for example at traffic lights, the automatic start-stop system can reduce noise pollution and also save up to 15% fuel.
answerASource: - fuel consumption
answerBSource: - noise pollution
answerCSource: - tyre wear

questionTextSource: You are driving a car with a plug-in hybrid drive system. How can you optimise fuel consumption?
explanationSource:
By driving proactively - with gentle acceleration and timely braking - you can reduce your vehicle's fuel consumption. The electric motor is often used in this driving style. 
You can also reduce fuel consumption by regularly charging the battery of your plug-in hybrid on the power grid or minimize it by driving purely electrically.
answerASource: I regularly charge the battery using the mains supply
answerBSource: I use the electric drive through my driving style
answerCSource: I only use the recuperation to charge the battery

questionTextSource: How can you avoid unnecessary noise?
explanationSource: Unnecessary noise you avoid by properly storing or use of the tension chains and by the renewing of knocked-out tailgate hinges or closures as they can rattle loudly.
answerASource: By replacing worn sideboard hinges or fastenings
answerBSource: Through correct stowing or use of chains
answerCSource: By greatly increasing the tyre pressure of an empty vehicle

questionTextSource: What can cause noise pollution?
explanationSource: 3 answers are correct. Loose or worn out tailgate hinges and unsecured load, but also loose tension chains.
answerASource: Loose chains
answerBSource: Loose or worn sideboard hinges
answerCSource: Unsecured load

questionTextSource: How can you avoid noise in built-up areas, in particular with empty vehicles?
explanationSource: In town you can avoid particularly poor road with your empty vehicle noise by inappropriate speed.
answerASource: By adapting your speed, particularly on a bad road surface
answerBSource: By driving at high speed in a low gear
answerCSource: By changing gear frequently with double-clutching

questionTextSource: Which motor vehicles are allowed to use a road with this sign?
explanationSource: This road leads in an environmental zone. All buses with a corresponding emissions sticker and all trucks with a corresponding sticker are allowed to drive here. Not all cars have this plaque, so are "all car" wrong.
answerASource: Any lorry with a corresponding emissions sticker
answerBSource: Any bus with a corresponding emissions sticker
answerCSource: Any car

questionTextSource: How can you avoid creating noise?
explanationSource: Noise you can prevent this by you as possible befüllst empty compressed air tanks from a stationary plant and if you exchange worn out lift closures.
answerASource: - filling empty compressed air tanks from a stationary facility, if possible
answerBSource: - replacing worn-out side latches
answerCSource: - allowing the engine to idle during longer waiting times

questionTextSource: When planning your journeys, how can you make them environmentally-friendlier?
explanationSource: For the sake of the environment, motorists should refrain from all trips that are not absolutely necessary. Therefore, plan your trips well and make carpools as often as possible to avoid unnecessary trips. Another positive contribution to active environmental protection is the use of public transport.
answerASource: - combining several destinations into one single trip
answerBSource: - forming car pools with other vehicle users
answerCSource: - planning to use public transport instead of my own vehicle

questionTextSource: How do you drive down a descending gradient safely?
explanationSource: On a down grade, use the engine brake by shifting down to a lower gear. This relieves the service brakes. Under no circumstances change to idle! Because idling puts the engine brake out of action, thus the service brakes could overheat. In addition, the brake booster cannot work when the ignition is switched off. This considerably lengthens the braking distance.
answerASource: - select a gear in which I am not required to brake a great deal
answerBSource: - drive in neutral
answerCSource: - switch the ignition off

questionTextSource: What motor vehicles are permitted to enter an environment zone without a fine-particle badge?
explanationSource: All two- and three-wheeled motor vehicles and agricultural and forestry tractors may enter an environmental zone without the carbon emission sticker. They are exempt from the restrictions of the environmental zone. The installation of a catalytic converter does not exempt you as a car driver from the obligation to obtain an environmental badge.
answerASource: All two and three-wheeled motor vehicles
answerBSource: All agricultural and forestry tractors
answerCSource: All motor vehicles with a catalytic converter

questionTextSource: What manufacturer specifications will allow you to know if your motor vehicle is environmentally-friendly?
explanationSource: The emission class provides information about the emission of pollutants. The emission classes defined by the standard and/or the law subdivide products that emit exhaust emissions or interference signals. In the automotive sector, moreover, the following applies: The lower the fuel consumption, the higher the efficiency class and thus the environmental friendliness of the vehicle.
answerASource: - about the fuel consumption
answerBSource: - about the efficiency category
answerCSource: - about the emissions class

questionTextSource: Why are short journeys performed with a cold engine especially damaging to the environment?
explanationSource: Short trips with a cold engine usually consume more fuel than driving with a motor in operating temperature. That's why they are particularly harmful to the environment. In addition, the friction is greater in short-distance driving with a cold engine. This leads to a higher material wear.
answerASource: - more fuel is consumed
answerBSource: - the level of material wear is higher
answerCSource: - the vehicle's battery will be over-charged

questionTextSource: What causes avoidable noise?
explanationSource: All noise disturbances arising during normal operation of a vehicle are considered unavoidable. As a car driver, you should avoid any excess noise, for example, honking as a greeting or closing the doors very loudly. A roof rack will not cause you any avoidable noise.
answerASource: By sounding the horn as a greeting
answerBSource: By firmly slamming doors shut
answerCSource: By driving with a roof-rack

questionTextSource: How does the eco-roll mode in commercial vehicles contribute to the reduction of fuel consumption?
explanationSource: Because the kinetic energy of the vehicle is maximally utilized in eco-roll mode is the right answer. This has to do with the automatic neutral position of the transmission. The rolling phase will not be shortened and the engine braking effect is not increased.
answerASource: - vehicle's kinetic energy is utilised to its maximum
answerBSource: - engine braking effect is increased
answerCSource: - vehicle's roll phase is reduced

questionTextSource: When must motorcycles be submitted for official inspection (Hauptuntersuchung)?
explanationSource: Similarly to cars, motorcycles should be inspected every two years
answerASource: Every two years
answerBSource: Every three years
answerCSource: Every time they are re-registered

questionTextSource: How can you tell when you need to bring your vehicle in for its next vehicle roadworthiness test?
explanationSource: The date for the next main inspection of your vehicle can be found in the certificate of registration part 1. In addition, the appointment is clearly visible on the inspection plate printed on your license plate.
answerASource: By looking at the inspection tag on the rear license plate
answerBSource: By reading the entry made in the registration certificate Part I
answerCSource: By reading the entry in the operating manual for my vehicle

questionTextSource: You have bought a second-hand car. The official inspection (Hauptuntersuchung) has just been carried out. When is it due for its next official inspection?
explanationSource: If the main inspection of a used car has just happened, you must do the next general inspection after two years. Only for newly purchased cars, the deadline for the next general inspection is three years.
answerASource: After two years
answerBSource: After one year
answerCSource: After three years

questionTextSource: You have left your registered caravan trailer for six months during the winter on a campground. What applies as far as the official inspection (Hauptuntersuchung) is concerned?
explanationSource: Even if you have parked your caravan on a campsite, you must meet the deadline for the general inspection. Technical defects, such as rust, can occur at any time and become a safety hazard if they're not repaired.
answerASource: The date indicated on the test stamp must be observed
answerBSource: The date indicated on the test stamp is extended by the time the caravan was stationary on the campground

questionTextSource: You have been requested to present a truck with a gross vehicle weight rating of 18 t for its vehicle roadworthiness test. What vehicle documents do you need?

answerBSource: Inspection log book
answerCSource: Registration certificate Part II (Motor Vehicle Title)

questionTextSource: What alterations can result in the permit for use (Betriebserlaubnis) being cancelled?
explanationSource: Adding a sidecar or fitting tires of a different size impact the way your vehicle behave and will thus lead the cancelation of your permit for use. Fitting a rear fog lamp does not change the way your vehicle behave and thus is permitted.

answerBSource: Fitting tyres of a different size
answerCSource: Fitting a rear fog lamp

questionTextSource: What alterations can result in the permit for use (Betriebserlaubnis) being cancelled?
explanationSource: Your vehicule's next roadworthiness test can be seen either in the Part I of the registration certificate or on the inspection tag that is on your rear license plate
answerASource: Fitting an engine of different power
answerBSource: Fitting fairings
answerCSource: Fitting a radio

questionTextSource: What alterations can result in the permit for use (Betriebserlaubnis) being cancelled?
explanationSource: Changing the gear ratio or the engine power impact the way your vehicle behave and will thus lead the cancelation of your permit for use
answerASource: Alterations to the gear ratio
answerBSource: Alterations to the engine to increase power
answerCSource: Fitting an electric starter

questionTextSource: You are holding a driving permit class A1. Which of the vehicles described are you allowed to drive?
explanationSource: With a A1 driving permit you can ride a vehicle that has a 80km/h maximum speed, 120cm3 capacity and 11kW power

questionTextSource: What are the requirements when the motor liability insurance of your vehicle has expired?
explanationSource: It is required to have an insurance for your vehicle, without one you are not allowed to drive it

answerCSource: - must be presented for inspection at the technical test centre

questionTextSource: When are you not allowed to drive a motorized bicycle on public roads?
explanationSource: A motorized bicycle has to be insured to cover for third party damages in case of accidents and the vehicle needs to be roadworthy
answerASource: When you have no motor vehicle liability insurance
answerBSource: When the motorized bicycle is not roadworthy

questionTextSource: What are the consequences when driving a motorized bicycle which can run faster than 25 km/h because it has been tuned?
explanationSource: Tuning your motorized bicycle to go faster than 25km/h void your permit for use, insurance and you can be fined for driving without the right permit.

answerBSource: Fine and punishment because permit for use and insurance cover have lapsed

questionTextSource: What cancels the permit for use of a motorized bicycle?
explanationSource: Modifications that impact the behaviour of your motorcycle such as gear ratio, silencer, can result in the permit of use being canceled

answerBSource: Modifying the gear ratio

answerCSource: Evidence of insurance

questionTextSource: You want to drive on public roads. Which insurance must you take out for your vehicle?
explanationSource: You are required by law to have a motor vehicle liability insurance to drive your vehicle which covers damanges done to third parties in case of accidents
answerASource: Motor vehicle liability insurance
answerBSource: Complete vehicle insurance

questionTextSource: The insurance indicator on your two-wheeler is no longer valid. What are the consequences if you continue to drive it so?
explanationSource: It is legally required to have an insurance to drive. Without one you are at risk of a fine or imprisonment. If you have an accident without an insurance, you will have to pay for any third party damage yourself
answerASource: I must reckon with a fine or imprisonment
answerBSource: I will personally have to bear the costs of any accidental damage that occurs
answerCSource: I may only drive at walking speed

questionTextSource: What documents must you carry with you, if you want to drive a motorbike with an engine size larger than 200 cm³?
explanationSource: To drive you need to have your driver's license and the part I of your registration certificate to prove that both you and the motorcycle are authorised to drive
answerASource: My driving licence
answerBSource: The registration certificate Part I (Motor Vehicle Registration)

questionTextSource: What documents do you have to carry with you, if you want to drive a light motorcycle?
explanationSource: To drive you need to have your driver's license and the part I of your registration certificate to prove that both you and the motorcycle are authorised to drive
answerASource: My driving licence
answerBSource: The operating licence or the registration certificate Part I (Motor Vehicle Registration)
answerCSource: The registration certificate Part II (Motor Vehicle Title)

questionTextSource: What documents do you have to carry with you, if you want to drive a small-engined motorcycle?
explanationSource: You are required to have all three documents, your driver's license, an insurance certificate and an operating license or certificate of conformity
answerASource: My driving licence

questionTextSource: What documents do you have to carry with you, if you want to drive a compulsory registrable vehicle?
explanationSource: As a car driver, you must always have your driver's license and vehicle registration certificate (certificate of registration part 1) with you. explanationSource: Coming soon...

questionTextSource: A technical modification to your vehicle means that the operating licence is extinguished. What consequences should you anticipate if you nevertheless use the vehicle on public roads?
explanationSource: If you use your vehicle on public roads despite the expired operating license, you will lose your insurance cover because the vehicle is no longer the one that was originally insured. Since there is an obligation to have insurance in Germany, driving without insurance is a misdemeanor. In the event of a police check, a fine will be imposed and you must expect an entry in the register of fitness to drive.
answerASource: A public authority fine
answerBSource: An entry into the Central Register of Road Traffic Offenders
answerCSource: The loss of my insurance cover

questionTextSource: What type of trailers used in agriculture and forestry do not have to have their own registration number?

answerCSource: - that may travel in excess of 25 km/h

questionTextSource: You want to transport hazardous goods, but have more than the permissible exempted allowance. What conditions must you fulfil in order to transport the goods?

answerBSource: - carry an ADR Certificate with me

questionTextSource: What is the meaning of the numbers on a sign of this kind?

questionTextSource: You would like to drive a truck with a gross vehicle weight rating of 8.0 t, which is carrying orange warning plates. Which of the following conditions do you have to fulfil?
explanationSource: With your truck with 8.0 t permissible gross weight, you must meet the following conditions: You have (hazardous materials certification) carry and possess a driving license class C an ADR certificate. Accident freedom has nothing to do.

questionTextSource: An inspection has been performed because of a technical modification made to your vehicle. What are you obliged to do?
explanationSource: After the technical modification of a vehicle, an acceptance report is usually created during the assessment. However, an entry may also be made in part 1 of the certificate of registration. The protocol must be carried by the vehicle driver during each journey. On request, the protocol must be presented, for example, during a stop-and-search operation.

answerBSource: - forward the inspection report or confirmation to the vehicle manufacturer

questionTextSource: The maximum design speed of a moped exceeds 25 km/h due to a prohibited technical modification. What are the consequences for me if I ride such a moped on public roads?
explanationSource: As soon as you change anything on your vehicle without a general operating permit or approval, the operating permit expires. Since riding a moped over 25 km/h is already class AM or higher, you are riding without a valid driving licence.

answerBSource: I am committing an administrative offence because I have no authorisation to operate the moped

questionTextSource: An assessment is required following modifications to your vehicle. When must this be performed?

questionTextSource: What technical modification to a motorbike will generally void the issued operating licence?
explanationSource: The original gear ratio of the pinion and sprocket is part of the operating license granted. All changes that affect the drive of your vehicle must be registered. This means that the operating permit expires if you change the transmission ratio.

answerBSource: Any change to the engine power
answerCSource: Installing a tankbag

questionTextSource: What technical modifications to a two-wheeler could result in the withdrawal of the vehicle’s type approval?
explanationSource:
The operating licence expires if modifications are made to the vehicle that change the vehicle type, could cause a hazard or worsen the exhaust or noise values.

questionTextSource: What is the latest date on which you must display the new proof of insurance on your two-wheeler in order to continue driving without any gap in your cover?

questionTextSource: The validity of the insurance plate on your bike has expired. What are the consequences for its use on public roads?
explanationSource:
It is forbidden to use a two-wheeler on public roads without a valid insurance license plate. The law requires compulsory insurance so that possible accident costs are covered. 
You are allowed to push your two-wheeler on a sidewalk without a valid insurance license plate, as this does not count as “driving”.

questionTextSource: The suspension of your motor vehicle has been lowered and an expert opinion must be obtained. When is this required?

questionTextSource: You are holding a driving permit class B. Your car has the following specifications: - empty mass 1900 kg - permissible total mass 2400 kg - permissible towed load 1500 kg Which trailer are you allowed to tow?

When the car is fully loaded, the loaded trailer must not weigh more than 1,100 kg. Exception: The car has a maximum permissible mass of 3,500 kg and the trailer weighs less than 750 kg.

questionTextSource: You are holding a driving permit class C. Your truck has the following specifications: - empty mass 9000 kg - permissible total mass 18000 kg - permissible towed load 22000 kg Which trailer are you allowed to tow?

As a truck driver in commercial freight transport, you must have the following documents with you:

Carrying these documents is required by law and serves to ensure orderly and safe road transport.

questionTextSource: What can affect the driving stability of a towed trailer?
explanationSource:
The load of a trailer should be distributed so that the center of gravity is as close as possible to the axle of the trailer. This ensures optimal driving stability and controllability of the trailer.
A load center of gravity that is far back can have a serious impact on the driving stability of your trailer. This can cause the trailer to sway more easily during sudden steering movements or at higher speeds, increasing the risk of an accident.
If the tire pressure on your trailer is too low, it can affect the driving stability of your trailer. On the one hand, low tire pressure leads to poorer road grip, which makes your trailer more uncontrollable when cornering. On the other hand, low tire pressure leads to increased tire wear, which increases the risk of tire damage while driving.
A shock absorber that is already leaking can also have a negative impact on the driving stability of your trailer. The leaking shock absorber on your trailer loses its damping properties, which can lead to unstable driving behavior and dangerous situations that are difficult to control.
answerASource: A centre of gravity that is far back in the trailer
answerBSource: Insufficient tyre pressure in the trailer tyres
answerCSource: A leaking shock absorber

If you brake sharply while turning, a torque force would tilt you from leaning to one side to a more upward position which will increase the radius of your turn

answerCSource: If driving straight ahead, driving stability is increased in any case
